because i want to solve for cross session awarness and reach as part of my investigtations i suspect that performing somehting with the logs outside of where claude code governs storage and trim may become valuiable. i am also wondering what happens if we push these last few assessments and questions through the same opus to fable ladder and include context around cross session material wherever we left that., here are note from two ohter sessions for ecxmple that i can't tell fi they are still real or resolved

What's left in the backlog
Lower-ranked / strategic signals from the triage — none are catch-net-shaped:

behavioral-setpoint control loop (L2, explicitly a Brien-greenlight call — biggest build)
2026-05-31 positioning cluster (4 active strategy signals)
always-on-hosting / agent-realtime-observability (L2, infrastructure-decision)
the 22 pre-existing premature-resolved signals (a separate closure-discipline cleanup)
These need your direction (or a greenlight) rather than autonomous execution. Point me at one whenever you're ready.a


TL;DR: Yes, and today handed you the case study: the residue was a missing transaction boundary, the race was a missing liveness channel, and I diagnosed the sibling session with ps aux, which means process-table archaeology is currently your cross-session awareness protocol. Both patterns you named are real, but they are two different primitives, and the "transcendental" version emerges from composing them cheaply, not from building shared omniscience. Signal captured to Loom: SIG-LOOM-LIVENESS-GAP-ps-aux-was-the-protocol-2026-07-02, committed and pushed.

Status: both chips still executing; no collision risk between them (different file surfaces). Loom signal landed at dd8ebce.

Your move: if the operations-lease atom below reads right, it is a natural next Loom increment (an extension of observe, or an 8th verb candidate).

The architecture read, in your terms:

Work branching is spatial isolation. Chips already run in fresh worktrees, which is why three of them landed on cast today without stepping on each other. The remaining exposure is that shared product repos get mutated on main's live working tree by pipelines and long-lived sessions alike. Branching eliminates write-write races, but note what it does not buy you: it defers semantic conflicts to merge time, and it does nothing for read-side races. My verification suite was a reader that got corrupted by a concurrent writer; a worktree would have protected my read but also frozen it against exactly the fresh state I was trying to verify. Isolation is necessary, not sufficient.

The jsonl awareness pattern is temporal coordination, and the substrate mostly exists. Trace-archive jsonl, .intent/events.jsonl, and Loom priming are already an event fabric. What is missing is precise and small: liveness. Nothing lets a session declare "I am running the suite in cast for the next two minutes" and nothing consults such declarations at decision points. Loom primes at session birth; mid-session decisions fly blind. That is the gap between orientation and coordination.

The smallest useful atom: a repo operations lease. One append-only jsonl stream per shared repo where sessions append op-start and op-end events (op class, session id, TTL heartbeat), plus a check helper consulted before suite runs and bulk writes. TTL expiry means a crashed session never wedges the repo. It rides the same trace fabric the code-owned-landing chip is already extending, so the two compose: landing events and lease events on one stream give you causality and liveness together.

On the transcendental framing, one first-principles sharpening. Coordination at AI speed does not come from every session knowing everything; that is an O(n squared) context tax, and your own Coherence Engineering axioms treat coordination as a tax to invest deliberately. Distributed systems solved this shape decades ago: you do not need telepathy, you need a lock, a log, and a bus. Leases for mutual exclusion, append-only streams for causal ordering, git as the durable serialization point, priming for orientation. The emergent property you are pointing at, coherence across autonomous sessions, falls out of those four primitives placed at the right seams. Build the primitives; the awareness is what running them feels like from the inside.

One housekeeping note from the signal commit: loom has the same untracked .entire/ session-metadata residue cast had. Same one-line gitignore fix applies; I left it alone since the pattern question of whether to sweep all product repos at once belongs with the overwatch residue check that is currently building.

**TL;DR:** Both of your pasted session notes are real and 100% unresolved, nothing in them was ever executed, and your suspicion about logs outside Claude Code's governance just paid off concretely: Entire has been silently checkpointing full session transcripts into git shadow branches since at least June 9, 117 transcripts currently preserved there as git objects immune to the 7-day trim, and 23 of the 108 rotated-out sessions behind today's recall analysis are recoverable from it after all (85 remain permanently gone, revised down from my earlier "all of them" claim). **Status:** the Opus rung of the ladder is running now, three parallel grounding tracks. **Your move:** nothing yet, synthesis lands when they return.

What the quick census established before launching:

**Note A (backlog):** every item still open. Behavioral-setpoint control loop, status captured, untouched since June 15. Always-on-hosting, status captured, untouched since May 16. The positioning cluster is actually 5 signals, not 4, all from 2026-05-31. And the premature-resolved cleanup is 18 signals across 9 files per the roadready closure signal's own count, deferral explicitly recorded, remediation never executed.

**Note B (Loom liveness):** the signal is real and committed at dd8ebce exactly as the note claimed. But the operations-lease atom it proposed was never built, and even the one-line .entire gitignore fix it flagged as trivial is still sitting there, .entire/ still untracked in loom right now.

So the pattern from earlier today now has four confirmed instances: Layer 4.2's promote decision surfaced twice unanswered, your two session notes evaporated, and Witness signals from June 8 show some session already discovered the Entire transcript seam and that discovery also went nowhere. The system's handoffs are honest and durable on disk, and structurally undelivered. That's the actual cross-session awareness problem, and it's narrower and more fixable than shared omniscience: the write side works, the read side doesn't exist.

The three Opus tracks now grounding this:

1. **Transcript preservation:** what the June 8-9 Witness signals already designed for Entire ingest, and the shortest path to a harvest that lands transcripts somewhere Claude Code's trim never touches, including the NDA/redaction boundary question since engagement content rides in those transcripts.
2. **Pending-decision census:** sweep every product's signal stream for items gated on you with no recorded disposition, and build the actual register, one row per open decision with age and what stalls while it waits. This turns "I can't tell if these notes are still real" from a per-note archaeology exercise into a queryable surface.
3. **Liveness channel:** whether entire status (which already lists every active session machine-wide with prompt, activity time, and token count, I verified it working today) plus Loom's existing session registry means the ops-lease from note B is mostly built already and needs only a heartbeat verb, versus building the jsonl lease as specced.

Fable synthesis happens here in the main loop when they return, with the full arc of today in context rather than a 400-word compressed brief.

# Track findings: transcript preservation architecture (Witness)

## Q1 — What the June 8-9 signals already knew; was ongoing-ingest proposed; status; did anything execute

**Yes — ongoing ingest of session transcripts into Witness before rotation was already explicitly proposed, and the mechanism was already built. What is missing is only the automated trigger.** The June signals are startlingly close to today's stated goal; this seam is not new ground.

What the signals establish (with citations):

- **`SIG-ENTIRE-CAPTURE-READOUT-2026-06-08.md`** (lines 16-54) quantified the corpus: Entire captures "full replayable Claude Code session transcripts" — 134 sessions / 7,593 records / 17.2M in `Core/products/library-index/.entire` as of 2026-06-08. It named the exact record taxonomy (assistant/user turns, `toolUseResult`, attachments, hook telemetry). It shipped the federation adapter `src/ingest_entire.py` (line 44) as a sibling to `ingest_jsonl`/`ingest_otlp`, TDD-tested (6 tests, suite 71 green), demonstrated on real data (736/736 records store-valid). The conservation design is stated at line 47: **`.entire` keeps the raw verbatim; Witness keeps a lightweight projection with a `sessionId`+`uuid` back-pointer** (no merge, one record → one event).

- **`SIG-WITNESS-ENTIRE-ONGOING-INGEST-2026-06-08.md`** is the exact proposal you're now re-deriving. Line 17: "no automated trigger for ongoing library-index `.entire` ingestion." Lines 29-38 lay out three options — **(A)** add to a Witness watcher (`src/watcher.py`), **(B)** a `mcp__scheduled-tasks` cron, **(C)** manual cadence. Status: **`upstream-pending`**, re-triaged 2026-06-17 as "still genuinely open," verified on disk — no cron/watcher/scheduled-task drives `python -m src.ingest_entire`. The 2026-06-17 triage note (lines 11-15) parks it deliberately: the resolve trigger is "Brien wants fresh corpus coverage" or ".entire backlog > ~500 sessions." **Your stated goal this turn is precisely that trigger firing.**

- **`SIG-FRICTION-CC-TRANSCRIPT-CENSUS-2026-06-09.md`** is the more important discovery for your goal. It found (lines 30-38) that **Claude Code already writes every hook fire into its own transcript at `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session>.jsonl`** and built a **PULL reader** — `src/ingest_cc_transcript.py` — that reads those native CC transcripts directly. Critical caveat I verified in the code: that reader is currently **read-only for friction analysis** (its docstring, lines 21-23: "no Witness store needed for the friction analysis itself"). **It reads CC's native transcripts but does not persist them into the Witness store.** The reader-over-CC-transcripts machinery exists; it just isn't wired to `events_store.append`.

- **`SIG-WITNESS-ENTIRE-DUPLICATE-INVARIANT-2026-06-08.md`**: flagged the catch-net gap — no `chain_audit` invariant (`I-ENTIRE-IO-NO-DUPLICATE-SESSION`) asserts post-hoc that no `sessionId` appears more than its record-count in the store. Status `upstream-pending`; the in-code idempotency guard (`ingest_entire.ingest_store(skip_existing=True)`, verified lines 138-152) covers re-run no-op but not store-state.

- **`SPEC-WITNESS-EVENTS-JSONL-ADAPTER-001.md`** designs the OTLP-JSON ingest contract with an explicit **degraded-mode + catchup** design (lines 164-179): products emit locally, files accumulate with no data loss, Witness reads pre-existing files in catchup mode when it comes online, emits `SIG-WITNESS-CATCHUP-COMPLETE`. This is the durability-before-consumption pattern already specified.

**Did anything execute?** Yes, more than the signals claim: I verified the Witness store on disk (`farm/events-store/2026-06-08.jsonl`, 5.1MB) already holds **7,593 `entire-io` events** — the bulk `ingest_store` backfill that the readout signal (line 32, 50) described as "still pending" **did land**. What did NOT execute: any automation. No launchd (`~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.brien.*` — none reference witness/entire/ingest), no cron, no watcher (`src/watcher.py` does not exist). The backfill was a one-time manual run.

## Q2 — Shortest path to closing the loop

**Neither (a) nor (b) as framed. The answer is (c): the specs already designed the better mechanism, and one of the two adapters is already built and battle-tested against the exact file path you care about.** But the design needs one correction the signals didn't fully resolve.

The decisive fact: **`src/ingest_entire.py` reads `<entire_root>/metadata/<uuid>/full.jsonl`** (verified, line 82: `full = Path(session_dir) / "full.jsonl"`). That is *exactly* the shadow-branch path in today's VERIFIED FACTS (`.entire/metadata/<session-id>/full.jsonl`). The adapter that de-strands Entire transcripts into an append-only store already targets the precise artifact that survives Claude Code's 7-day trim. You are not building a pipeline; you are scheduling one that exists.

Why not (b) "extend Entire + never prune shadow branches": Entire's shadow branches are **git objects immune to CC trim** (the strength you found), but they are (1) opaque to query — you cannot run `witness query --source entire-io` against a refs/heads/entire/* branch, (2) coupled to the repo's git lifecycle (a repo GC or a clone-without-shadow-refs loses them), and (3) they do NOT cover the interactive mount sessions that write to `~/.claude/projects` but never get an Entire checkpoint unless the mount hooks fire. Keeping shadow branches is necessary belt-and-suspenders but insufficient as *the* store — it gives you preservation without reach, and reach is half your stated goal.

   `WITNESS_ROOT=/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness python -m src.ingest_entire /Users/brien/Workspaces/.entire` (the mount store) **and** `.../library-index/.entire`. The `skip_existing=True` idempotency guard (verified, lines 138-152) makes daily runs safe. This is L4 per your grants (scheduled-task creation, Workspaces-local) — no new infrastructure, ~10 lines of scheduler config.

2. **Add a store-append verb to the CC-transcript reader (closes reach — the interactive-mount sessions Entire misses).** `ingest_cc_transcript.py` already globs `~/.claude/projects/<enc-cwd>/*.jsonl` and parses records (verified, lines 62-73). The gap is one function: a `map_record

content.content:
1	That is the final decisive fact. `entire status` errors with "not a git repository" outside a repo — it is **repo-scoped, not machine-wide**. Yet when run from Workspaces it listed 18 sessions including scheduled-task and Cast-freshening sessions. So `entire status` shows sessions whose git operations touched *this repo* (or that entire tracks for this repo), which for Brien's Workspaces monorepo is effectively "everything," but it is NOT a machine-wide cross-repo view — it is per-repo, which actually matches the ops-lease's "per shared repo" scoping requirement well.
2	
3	I have everything I need. Let me write the analysis.
4	
5	## Findings
6	
7	**File citations for everything below:**
8	- Proposal: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/.intent/signals/SIG-LOOM-LIVENESS-GAP-ps-aux-was-the-protocol-2026-07-02.md`
9	- Loom substrate: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/src/loom.py` (Thread dataclass L92-118; `live()` L382-392; `siblings()` L396-459; `heartbeat()` L300-312; `write()` L316-350 — note it bumps `last_heartbeat`)
10	- Hooks: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/hooks/loom_session_start.py` (register/heartbeat L389-405; priming L257-321), `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/hooks/loom_session_end.py` (seal L246-280)
11	- Architecture: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/ARCHITECTURE.md` (§2 schema, §3 thread-per-writer, §6 process model)
12	- Live DB: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/.context/loom/loom.db`
13	
14	---
15	
16	### Q1. Does `entire status` cover what the ops-lease wanted? What does it NOT cover?
17	
18	`entire status` (v0.5.1, `/Users/brien/.local/bin/entire`) output shape, verified today:
19	
20	```
21	Claude Code · 50391708-4b10-40a0-b142-44a97d056501
22	> "because i want to solve for cross session awarness and re..."
23	started 3h ago · active 4m ago · tokens 21726.6k
24	```
25	
26	**COVERS (and better than Loom):**
27	- **Who is alive** — session id + session class (all "Claude Code"), with a *real* last-activity wall-clock ("active 4m ago"). This is the single thing Loom's DB gets wrong (see Q3).
28	- **Doing what** — the originating prompt, truncated (~52 chars). Enough to distinguish "intake-drain scheduled task" from "Cast freshening nightly" from an interactive session. The ops-lease wanted op-class; the prompt is a coarser but free substitute.
29	- **Where / when** — start time, token count (a proxy for how much work/how deep).
30	- **Repo scoping** — confirmed: run from `/tmp` it errors `✕ not a git repository`. It is **repo-scoped, not machine-wide.** Run from Workspaces it listed 18 sessions. This is actually *aligned* with the ops-lease's "one lease per shared repo" design, not a gap.
31	
32	**Does NOT cover (the real gaps):**
33	- **Op-class granularity** — "active 4m ago" tells you a session breathed 4 min ago; it does NOT tell you *"I am running the pytest suite in cast until ~T+2m"* or *"I am mid bulk-write to registry/."* The lease's whole point was a declared operation with a class and a target subtree. `entire status` has session-level liveness, not operation-level intent. This is the gap that would have prevented the cast suite race — two sessions both "active" tells you nothing about whether their *operations* collide.
34	- **TTL / crash semantics** — no lease expiry model. It infers liveness from activity timestamps, which is actually *better* than a TTL for the crash case (a crashed session simply stops showing "active"), but there is no notion of "this operation claims this resource until T."
35	- **Machine-readability** — `entire status --help` confirms only `--detailed`; **no `--json`.** Output is TUI prose with box-drawing glyphs and relative times ("4m ago"). A parser is possible but brittle (relative-time strings, truncated prompts, unstable formatting across versions).
36	- **Sub-repo targeting** — repo granularity, not subtree. Both the cast-suite session and a cast-registry-writer show up identically as Workspaces sessions.
37	
38	---
39	
40	### Q2 + Q3 (answered together, because Q3 changes the Q2 answer): Is the ops-lease 80% built on Loom's registry?
41	
42	**No — and this is the load-bearing finding. Loom's registry looks 80% built but is functionally broken for liveness, and `entire status` is the thing that actually works.**
43	
44	Loom's `Thread` (loom.py L92-104) already carries `session_id, session_class, cwd, engagement, product, opened_at, last_heartbeat, sealed_at`. `live()` (L382-392) and `siblings()` (L396-459) already project "who is alive, scoped by engagement/product/cwd." On paper that is the ops-lease read-path minus op-class. The signal's instinct ("substrate already exists in pieces") is right about the *shape*.
45	
46	But the live DB falsifies it:
47	- **1888 threads, record kinds: `selvedge 1221, heartbeat 149, result 146, decision 1, question 1`.** Sessions register and seal; they almost never write anything mid-session.
48	- **The heartbeat is never re-fired.** The SessionStart hook registers (which stamps `last_heartbeat = opened_at`) and only heartbeats on a *re-fire* (resume/compact — `loom_session_start.py` L389-405). `heartbeat()` and `write()` both bump `last_heartbeat` (loom.py L306, L347), but nothing in the hook fabric calls them during normal work. Proof from the DB: this very session `50391708` shows `opened_at 17:12:43 == last_heartbeat 17:12:43`. The 189M-token session `af5baefe`, which `entire status` reports as "active 19h ago," has its last Loom record at 20:58 and only 2 results total.
49	- **Consequence:** Loom's `live()` returns "unsealed AND heartbeat within 120s" (L382-384). Because heartbeat never re-fires, a session is "live" in Loom only for the first 120 seconds after it registers, then silently drops out of `live()` while still running for hours. Loom's liveness window is 2 minutes wide on a substrate whose sessions run for hours. **Loom cannot answer "who is working right now."** `entire status` can, for free, because it derives activity from actual hook/commit events, not from a heartbeat nobody sends.
50	
51	So the ops-lease is **not 80% built on Loom** — it is ~50% built and the missing 50% is the hard, always-fails-in-practice half (a heartbeat that reliably fires mid-session, plus op-class, plus a consult-before-write check). Meanwhile `entire status` delivers the liveness half already, verified working, maintained by someone else.
52	
53	**Weighing the three increments:**
54	
55	| | (a) Parse `entire status` as read-path + Loom hooks as write-path | (b) Build ops-lease jsonl as specced | (c) Extend Loom registry w/ heartbeat + op-class |
56	|---|---|---|---|
57	| **Maintenance** | Low code, but brittle: no `--json`, relative-time + truncated-prompt parsing breaks on entire version bumps | New append-only file, new check helper, TTL sweeper — all Brien-owned forever | Medium: add one verb + a real heartbeat trigger to hooks already in the fabric |
58	| **Failure modes** | entire changes output format; parser silently wrong | crashed session wedges a lease unless TTL correct (the exact bug ops-lease worried about) | crashed session just goes stale by heartbeat window — same graceful expiry entire gets, no TTL bookkeeping |
59	| **Op-class** | absent (only prompt) | first-class | first-class (add `op_class` + `op_target` to a record) |
60	| **Who consumes** | sessions at decision points, Brien awareness surface | same | same, and it composes with existing priming/selvedge/Topography seam |
61	| **Liveness truth** | correct (entire's activity clock) | correct if heartbeat wired | correct only if heartbeat actually wired (the thing that's been missing) |
62	
63	---
64	
65	### Single recommendation
66	
67	**Do a hybrid, sequenced — not (a), (b), or (c) alone. Adopt `entire status` as the liveness *read-path* immediately (a-lite), and add op-class as a thin write-path on Loom (c-lite). Do NOT build the ops-lease jsonl (b) — it re-implements liveness that entire already gives you and re-introduces the TTL/crash-wedge bug entire avoids.**
68	
69	Concretely, smallest real increment, two moves:
70	
71	1. **Liveness read-path = `entire status`, parsed once, defensively.** Write one tiny wrapper (`loom/src/liveness.py` or a `who` verb) that shells `entire status`, parses the session blocks (id, prompt-snippet, started, active-ago, tokens), and returns structured rows. Guard it hard: fail-open to empty list, tolerate format drift, treat "active" times as advisory. This replaces `ps aux` archaeology (which we confirmed is useless anyway — PIDs are opaque `Claude Helper (Renderer)` processes with no session/prompt/repo mapping). Ask Brien to file with the entire team for a `--json` flag; until then the parser is the tax.
72	
73	2. **Op-class write-path = one new Loom record kind, fired at the two seams that actually race.** The ops-lease's real information gain over `entire status` is *operation intent* ("I'm running the suite / bulk-writing registry/ until ~T"). That is a single `RecordKind.OPERATION` (or reuse `RESULT` with a marker, matching the existing priming-ledger pattern at `loom_session_end.py` L121-122) carrying `{op_class, target_subtree, started, ttl_s}`, written at the start of a suite run or bulk write and cleared/re-written at end. A `check_operations(repo, target)` helper reads open operation records across live threads (join Loom's op-records against `entire status`'s liveness so a crashed session's stale op is ignored — **entire's activity clock is the crash-detector, replacing the TTL sweeper the ops-lease would have needed**). This is the piece that would actually have caught the 2026-07-02 cast double-suite race.
74	
75	**Why this over the pure options:** (b) is rejected because it rebuilds liveness Loom+entire already have and owns a TTL crash-model that entire gives free. Pure (a) is rejected because prompt-snippet is too coarse to prevent operation collisions — it has no op-class. Pure (c) is rejected because wiring a *reliable* mid-session heartbeat into Loom is exactly the thing that has never worked (149 heartbeats across 1888 threads proves it), so leaning on Loom's own `last_heartbeat` for liveness repeats a known failure; let entire own liveness, let Loom own only the declared-operation record.
76	
77	**One caveat to flag to Brien:** this leans on a third-party CLI (`entire`, v0.5.1) as a runtime dependency of the coordination fabric. That is acceptable because it fails open (no entire → fall back to Loom's degraded liveness → no worse than today) and because entire is already checkpointing the transcripts you depend on for the *other* track (the 117 shadow-branch transcripts). But it should be a stated, logged dependency, not an implicit one. The `.entire` gitignore residue the signal mentioned is partially real: `.entire` is ignored at the Workspaces root (`.gitignore:6:/.*`), but **`Core/products/loom/.entire/` is NOT ignored** (confirmed via `git check-ignore`) — that residue is still open and should be added to loom's `.gitignore`.

file.content:
That is the final decisive fact. `entire status` errors with "not a git repository" outside a repo — it is **repo-scoped, not machine-wide**. Yet when run from Workspaces it listed 18 sessions including scheduled-task and Cast-freshening sessions. So `entire status` shows sessions whose git operations touched *this repo* (or that entire tracks for this repo), which for Brien's Workspaces monorepo is effectively "everything," but it is NOT a machine-wide cross-repo view — it is per-repo, which actually matches the ops-lease's "per shared repo" scoping requirement well.

I have everything I need. Let me write the analysis.

## Findings

**File citations for everything below:**
- Proposal: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/.intent/signals/SIG-LOOM-LIVENESS-GAP-ps-aux-was-the-protocol-2026-07-02.md`
- Loom substrate: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/src/loom.py` (Thread dataclass L92-118; `live()` L382-392; `siblings()` L396-459; `heartbeat()` L300-312; `write()` L316-350 — note it bumps `last_heartbeat`)
- Hooks: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/hooks/loom_session_start.py` (register/heartbeat L389-405; priming L257-321), `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/hooks/loom_session_end.py` (seal L246-280)
- Architecture: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/loom/ARCHITECTURE.md` (§2 schema, §3 thread-per-writer, §6 process model)
- Live DB: `/Users/brien/Workspaces/.context/loom/loom.db`

---

### Q1. Does `entire status` cover what the ops-lease wanted? What does it NOT cover?

`entire status` (v0.5.1, `/Users/brien/.local/bin/entire`) output shape, verified today:

```
Claude Code · 50391708-4b10-40a0-b142-44a97d056501
> "because i want to solve for cross session awarness and re..."
started 3h ago · active 4m ago · tokens 21726.6k
```

**COVERS (and better than Loom):**
- **Who is alive** — session id + session class (all "Claude Code"), with a *real* last-activity wall-clock ("active 4m ago"). This is the single thing Loom's DB gets wrong (see Q3).
- **Doing what** — the originating prompt, truncated (~52 chars). Enough to distinguish "intake-drain scheduled task" from "Cast freshening nightly" from an interactive session. The ops-lease wanted op-class; the prompt is a coarser but free substitute.
- **Where / when** — start time, token count (a proxy for how much work/how deep).
- **Repo scoping** — confirmed: run from `/tmp` it errors `✕ not a git repository`. It is **repo-scoped, not machine-wide.** Run from Workspaces it listed 18 sessions. This is actually *aligned* with the ops-lease's "one lease per shared repo" design, not a gap.

**Does NOT cover (the real gaps):**
- **Op-class granularity** — "active 4m ago" tells you a session breathed 4 min ago; it does NOT tell you *"I am running the pytest suite in cast until ~T+2m"* or *"I am mid bulk-write to registry/."* The lease's whole point was a declared operation with a class and a target subtree. `entire status` has session-level liveness, not operation-level intent. This is the gap that would have prevented the cast suite race — two sessions both "active" tells you nothing about whether their *operations* collide.
- **TTL / crash semantics** — no lease expiry model. It infers liveness from activity timestamps, which is actually *better* than a TTL for the crash case (a crashed session simply stops showing "active"), but there is no notion of "this operation claims this resource until T."
- **Machine-readability** — `entire status --help` confirms only `--detailed`; **no `--json`.** Output is TUI prose with box-drawing glyphs and relative times ("4m ago"). A parser is possible but brittle (relative-time strings, truncated prompts, unstable formatting across versions).
- **Sub-repo targeting** — repo granularity, not subtree. Both the cast-suite session and a cast-registry-writer show up identically as Workspaces sessions.

---

### Q2 + Q3 (answered together, because Q3 changes the Q2 answer): Is the ops-lease 80% built on Loom's registry?

**No — and this is the load-bearing finding. Loom's registry looks 80% built but is functionally broken for liveness, and `entire status` is the thing that actually works.**

Loom's `Thread` (loom.py L92-104) already carries `session_id, session_class, cwd, engagement, product, opened_at, last_heartbeat, sealed_at`. `live()` (L382-392) and `siblings()` (L396-459) already project "who is alive, scoped by engagement/product/cwd." On paper that is the ops-lease read-path minus op-class. The signal's instinct ("substrate already exists in pieces") is right about the *shape*.

But the live DB falsifies it:
- **1888 threads, record kinds: `selvedge 1221, heartbeat 149, result 146, decision 1, question 1`.** Sessions register and seal; they almost never write anything mid-session.
- **The heartbeat is never re-fired.** The SessionStart hook registers (which stamps `last_heartbeat = opened_at`) and only heartbeats on a *re-fire* (resume/compact — `loom_session_start.py` L389-405). `heartbeat()` and `write()` both bump `last_heartbeat` (loom.py L306, L347), but nothing in the hook fabric calls them during normal work. Proof from the DB: this very session `50391708` shows `opened_at 17:12:43 == last_heartbeat 17:12:43`. The 189M-token session `af5baefe`, which `entire status` reports as "active 19h ago," has its last Loom record at 20:58 and only 2 results total.
- **Consequence:** Loom's `live()` returns "unsealed AND heartbeat within 120s" (L382-384). Because heartbeat never re-fires, a session is "live" in Loom only for the first 120 seconds after it registers, then silently drops out of `live()` while still running for hours. Loom's liveness window is 2 minutes wide on a substrate whose sessions run for hours. **Loom cannot answer "who is working right now."** `entire status` can, for free, because it derives activity from actual hook/commit events, not from a heartbeat nobody sends.

So the ops-lease is **not 80% built on Loom** — it is ~50% built and the missing 50% is the hard, always-fails-in-practice half (a heartbeat that reliably fires mid-session, plus op-class, plus a consult-before-write check). Meanwhile `entire status` delivers the liveness half already, verified working, maintained by someone else.

**Weighing the three increments:**

| | (a) Parse `entire status` as read-path + Loom hooks as write-path | (b) Build ops-lease jsonl as specced | (c) Extend Loom registry w/ heartbeat + op-class |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Maintenance** | Low code, but brittle: no `--json`, relative-time + truncated-prompt parsing breaks on entire version bumps | New append-only file, new check helper, TTL sweeper — all Brien-owned forever | Medium: add one verb + a real heartbeat trigger to hooks already in the fabric |
| **Failure modes** | entire changes output format; parser silently wrong | crashed session wedges a lease unless TTL correct (the exact bug ops-lease worried about) | crashed session just goes stale by heartbeat window — same graceful expiry entire gets, no TTL bookkeeping |
| **Op-class** | absent (only prompt) | first-class | first-class (add `op_class` + `op_target` to a record) |
| **Who consumes** | sessions at decision points, Brien awareness surface | same | same, and it composes with existing priming/selvedge/Topography seam |
| **Liveness truth** | correct (entire's activity clock) | correct if heartbeat wired | correct only if heartbeat actually wired (the thing that's been missing) |

---

### Single recommendation

**Do a hybrid, sequenced — not (a), (b), or (c) alone. Adopt `entire status` as the liveness *read-path* immediately (a-lite), and add op-class as a thin write-path on Loom (c-lite). Do NOT build the ops-lease jsonl (b) — it re-implements liveness that entire already gives you and re-introduces the TTL/crash-wedge bug entire avoids.**

Concretely, smallest real increment, two moves:

1. **Liveness read-path = `entire status`, parsed once, defensively.** Write one tiny wrapper (`loom/src/liveness.py` or a `who` verb) that shells `entire status`, parses the session blocks (id, prompt-snippet, started, active-ago, tokens), and returns structured rows. Guard it hard: fail-open to empty list, tolerate format drift, treat "active" times as advisory. This replaces `ps aux` archaeology (which we confirmed is useless anyway — PIDs are opaque `Claude Helper (Renderer)` processes with no session/prompt/repo mapping). Ask Brien to file with the entire team for a `--json` flag; until then the parser is the tax.

2. **Op-class write-path = one new Loom record kind, fired at the two seams that actually race.** The ops-lease's real information gain over `entire status` is *operation intent* ("I'm running the suite / bulk-writing registry/ until ~T"). That is a single `RecordKind.OPERATION` (or reuse `RESULT` with a marker, matching the existing priming-ledger pattern at `loom_session_end.py` L121-122) carrying `{op_class, target_subtree, started, ttl_s}`, written at the start of a suite run or bulk write and cleared/re-written at end. A `check_operations(repo, target)` helper reads open operation records across live threads (join Loom's op-records against `entire status`'s liveness so a crashed session's stale op is ignored — **entire's activity clock is the crash-detector, replacing the TTL sweeper the ops-lease would have needed**). This is the piece that would actually have caught the 2026-07-02 cast double-suite race.

**Why this over the pure options:** (b) is rejected because it rebuilds liveness Loom+entire already have and owns a TTL crash-model that entire gives free. Pure (a) is rejected because prompt-snippet is too coarse to prevent operation collisions — it has no op-class. Pure (c) is rejected because wiring a *reliable* mid-session heartbeat into Loom is exactly the thing that has never worked (149 heartbeats across 1888 threads proves it), so leaning on Loom's own `last_heartbeat` for liveness repeats a known failure; let entire own liveness, let Loom own only the declared-operation record.

**One caveat to flag to Brien:** this leans on a third-party CLI (`entire`, v0.5.1) as a runtime dependency of the coordination fabric. That is acceptable because it fails open (no entire → fall back to Loom's degraded liveness → no worse than today) and because entire is already checkpointing the transcripts you depend on for the *other* track (the 117 shadow-branch transcripts). But it should be a stated, logged dependency, not an implicit one. The `.entire` gitignore residue the signal mentioned is partially real: `.entire` is ignored at the Workspaces root (`.gitignore:6:/.*`), but **`Core/products/loom/.entire/` is NOT ignored** (confirmed via `git check-ignore`) — that residue is still open and should be added to loom's `.gitignore`.

content.content:
443	## 13. Product-Repo Working-Tree Residue Aging (governance catch-net)
444	
445	Guards the recompute-without-commit anti-pattern: an autonomous or session run of `--apply` tooling mutates hundreds of tracked files in a nested product repo and ends without committing, so large residue sits in the working tree with nothing flagging its age. **Worked example (SIG-RESIDUE-DISPOSITION-recompute-2026-07-01-committed-2026-07-02, cast):** a 2026-07-01 recompute session left 949 dirty entries across 900+ tracked files in `Core/products/cast`; the residue sat for 2 days, blocked clean git pulls, and forced every agent into staging workarounds before it was characterized and committed.
446	
447	**Executable check (main thread — sub-second per repo):**
448	
449	`Core/products/org-design-tooling/src/check-product-repo-residue.sh [products_root] [count_threshold] [age_hours]`
450	
451	- Default scan root: `~/Workspaces/Core/products` — every immediate child that is its own git repo (nested product repos per `reference_product_repos_nested`).
452	- **Flags a repo when EITHER** dirty entries (`git status --porcelain`) exceed 50, **OR** any dirty TRACKED file's mtime is older than 24h.
453	- **Exit 0** — clean. Prints the count of product repos scanned.
454	- **Exit 1** — RESIDUE: prints per flagged repo the dirty-entry count, aged-tracked count + oldest age, dominant path pattern, and suggested disposition.
455	- **Exit 2** — the scan could not run (bad root, git missing, no repos found). **Fails CLOSED** — treat as a finding, never a pass.
456	
457	**Surface in the status board:**
458	
459	```
460	| Governance: product-repo residue | ✅/⚠️/❌ | exit 0 = clean (N repos) · exit 1 = RESIDUE (N repos flagged) · exit 2 = scan failed (fail-closed) |
461	```
462	
463	Status map: exit 0 → ✅; exit 1 → ⚠️ and list each flagged repo in Issues Found under the label **[RES]** (Residue) with count, oldest age, and dominant path pattern; exit 2 → ❌ and surface the script's stderr in Issues Found.
464	
465	**Classification — surface only (catch-net, not write-through).** Do NOT auto-commit or auto-discard. The disposition protocol per the worked example: (1) characterize the residue — which run produced it (`repo-hygiene-sweep.py` classifies by source class A/B/C/D), is it byte-identical to what the tooling computes today (idempotency dry-run)?; (2) then commit-or-discard as ONE deliberate act, with a disposition signal. A single aged file is often class-D in-flight human work — judgment, not auto-flag-to-fix; the bulk-residue pattern (high count, uniform path prefix, tool-generated) is the real target. The durable upstream control remains producer-side write-through (`commit-outputs.sh` registration for the producing run) — when the same repo recurs across sweeps, that recurrence IS the signal to install it.
466	
467	**Signal-aware:** if a disposition signal with `status: resolved` (or an `active` in-flight-work signal) already covers a flagged repo's residue, elide or annotate per the standard signal-aware correlation table (same handling as Sections 9, 11, and 12).
468	
469	---
470	
471	Use parallel Agent spawns (model: "sonnet") for checks 1-4 and check 11 to minimize wall-clock time and preserve Opus budget for judgment. Run MCP probes in parallel in the main thread. Run usage tracking in main thread (file parsing, not judgment). Run governance checks (9a-9d) and the Section 12 intent-site and Section 13 product-repo-residue catch-nets in main thread (fast filesystem reads). Target: full sweep in under 90 seconds.

## 13. Product-Repo Working-Tree Residue Aging (governance catch-net)

Guards the recompute-without-commit anti-pattern: an autonomous or session run of `--apply` tooling mutates hundreds of tracked files in a nested product repo and ends without committing, so large residue sits in the working tree with nothing flagging its age. **Worked example (SIG-RESIDUE-DISPOSITION-recompute-2026-07-01-committed-2026-07-02, cast):** a 2026-07-01 recompute session left 949 dirty entries across 900+ tracked files in `Core/products/cast`; the residue sat for 2 days, blocked clean git pulls, and forced every agent into staging workarounds before it was characterized and committed.

**Executable check (main thread — sub-second per repo):**

`Core/products/org-design-tooling/src/check-product-repo-residue.sh [products_root] [count_threshold] [age_hours]`

- Default scan root: `~/Workspaces/Core/products` — every immediate child that is its own git repo (nested product repos per `reference_product_repos_nested`).
- **Flags a repo when EITHER** dirty entries (`git status --porcelain`) exceed 50, **OR** any dirty TRACKED file's mtime is older than 24h.
- **Exit 0** — clean. Prints the count of product repos scanned.
- **Exit 1** — RESIDUE: prints per flagged repo the dirty-entry count, aged-tracked count + oldest age, dominant path pattern, and suggested disposition.
- **Exit 2** — the scan could not run (bad root, git missing, no repos found). **Fails CLOSED** — treat as a finding, never a pass.

**Surface in the status board:**

```
| Governance: product-repo residue | ✅/⚠️/❌ | exit 0 = clean (N repos) · exit 1 = RESIDUE (N repos flagged) · exit 2 = scan failed (fail-closed) |
```

Status map: exit 0 → ✅; exit 1 → ⚠️ and list each flagged repo in Issues Found under the label **[RES]** (Residue) with count, oldest age, and dominant path pattern; exit 2 → ❌ and surface the script's stderr in Issues Found.

**Classification — surface only (catch-net, not write-through).** Do NOT auto-commit or auto-discard. The disposition protocol per the worked example: (1) characterize the residue — which run produced it (`repo-hygiene-sweep.py` classifies by source class A/B/C/D), is it byte-identical to what the tooling computes today (idempotency dry-run)?; (2) then commit-or-discard as ONE deliberate act, with a disposition signal. A single aged file is often class-D in-flight human work — judgment, not auto-flag-to-fix; the bulk-residue pattern (high count, uniform path prefix, tool-generated) is the real target. The durable upstream control remains producer-side write-through (`commit-outputs.sh` registration for the producing run) — when the same repo recurs across sweeps, that recurrence IS the signal to install it.

**Signal-aware:** if a disposition signal with `status: resolved` (or an `active` in-flight-work signal) already covers a flagged repo's residue, elide or annotate per the standard signal-aware correlation table (same handling as Sections 9, 11, and 12).

---

Use parallel Agent spawns (model: "sonnet") for checks 1-4 and check 11 to minimize wall-clock time and preserve Opus budget for judgment. Run MCP probes in parallel in the main thread. Run usage tracking in main thread (file parsing, not judgment). Run governance checks (9a-9d) and the Section 12 intent-site and Section 13 product-repo-residue catch-nets in main thread (fast filesystem reads). Target: full sweep in under 90 seconds.

## 14. Pending Brien-Gated Decisions (delivery catch-net)

Guards the undelivered-decision anti-pattern: correctly-gated L0/L2 asks get written as signal files that nothing routes to a surface Brien reads at session start, so they age silently. **Worked example (2026-07-03 census):** the Layer 4.2 promote/retire decision was surfaced 4 times (06-12, 06-28, 07-03 x2), each stamped decision_owner Brien, answered 0 times; two whole session-handoff backlogs evaporated the same way; the oldest open decision (fieldbook $25 exception) had aged 51 days. Register: 10 open items at build.

**Executable check (main thread, fast file reads):**

1. Read `.context/PENDING_DECISIONS.md` (the register, built 2026-07-03).
2. Freshness cross-check: grep the signal dirs (`.intent/signals/` at root plus `Core/frameworks/intent`, `Core/products/{loom,witness,parallax,cast,fieldbook}`) for files newer than the register's `updated:` date containing `decision_owner: Brien`, `L2` + `NOT executed|not taken|Brien.s call|greenlight`, or `status: captured` + decision language. Any hit not in the register = register drift, add the row (L4 write-through, update `updated:`).
3. For each register row, check the signal file's status field; if a disposition landed, delete the row (write-through) and note it.

**Surface in the status board:**

```
| Governance: pending decisions | ✅/⚠️/❌ | N open · oldest AGEd · M surfaced 2+ times unanswered |
```

Status map: 0 open → ✅; any open → ⚠️ with the top 3 by age listed in Issues Found under **[DEC]**, each as one line: age, level, one-line ask, path; any item surfaced 2+ times without disposition OR older than 45d → ❌ escalate to the top of the report, above Freshness Alerts. This section's entire purpose is delivery: the list must appear in the session-start report body itself, never only in this file.

---

Use parallel Agent spawns (model: "sonnet") for checks 1-4 and check 11 to minimize wall-clock time and preserve Opus budget for judgment. Run MCP probes in parallel in the main thread. Run usage tracking in main thread (file parsing, not judgment). Run governance checks (9a-9d) and the Section 12 intent-site, Section 13 product-repo-residue, and Section 14 pending-decisions catch-nets in main thread (fast filesystem reads). Target: full sweep in under 90 seconds.

input.description: Daily Witness ingest of Entire session transcripts before Claude Code's 7-day trim can lose them
input.prompt:
You are running the daily Witness transcript-preservation ingest. Context: Claude Code prunes session transcripts after ~7 days (cleanupPeriodDays: 7), and an audit on 2026-07-03 found 85 of 126 governance-relevant sessions permanently lost to that trim. The Entire CLI checkpoints full session transcripts to .entire/metadata/<session-id>/full.jsonl, and Witness has a built, tested, idempotent adapter for exactly that path. Your job is to run it against both Entire roots so transcripts land in Witness's append-only store (local, gitignored, infinite retention) regardless of Claude Code's rotation.

Steps:
1. Run: WITNESS_ROOT=/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness/.venv/bin/python -m src.ingest_entire /Users/brien/Workspaces/.entire
   (working directory must be /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness; if .venv/bin/python does not exist there, check the witness CLAUDE.md or README for the correct interpreter per the use-project-venv rule, and never use bare python3)
2. Run the same command against /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/library-index/.entire
3. The adapter is idempotent (skip_existing=True): re-ingesting already-stored sessions is a safe no-op. Record from each run's output: sessions seen, records ingested, records skipped.
4. Sanity check: confirm the Witness events-store grew or held steady (ls -la /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness/farm/events-store/), never shrank.
5. If either run errors: do NOT silently degrade. Append a one-line dated entry describing the exact failure (which root, which error) to /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/witness/.intent/signals/SIG-WITNESS-ENTIRE-ONGOING-INGEST-2026-06-08.md under a "## Run log" heading (create the heading if absent), since that signal is the tracking anchor for this automation.
6. On success, no signal and no commit is needed; this is a read-side harvest into a gitignored store. Keep output terse: one summary line per root.

Provenance: this task IS the executed Option B from SIG-WITNESS-ENTIRE-ONGOING-INGEST-2026-06-08 (Core/products/witness/.intent/signals/), whose parked resolve-trigger (Brien wants fresh corpus coverage) fired 2026-07-03 during the cross-session-awareness investigation.

# Cross-session awareness decomposes into three primitives; all three existed, none were connected

## The finding

Brien asked how to solve cross-session awareness and reach, suspecting that working the logs
outside Claude Code's storage governance would matter. A three-track Opus census plus main-loop
synthesis found the problem decomposes into preservation, liveness, and delivery, and that in every
case the mechanism already existed, was discovered by some prior session, and the discovery itself
then evaporated for lack of the third primitive (delivery). Five confirmed instances of the
evaporation pattern: Layer 4.2's promote decision (surfaced 4x, answered 0x), two session-handoff
backlogs Brien pasted verbatim because he could not tell if they were real, and the Witness/Entire
ingest seam (fully designed 2026-06-08, adapter built and tested, never scheduled).

## Preservation (executed today)

Three storage tiers with different governance: Claude Code transcripts (harness-governed, 7-day
trim, 85 of 126 governance-relevant sessions already permanently lost), Entire shadow branches
(git-governed, 117 full transcripts surviving as git objects, opaque to query), Witness events-store
(Brien-governed, append-only, queryable, and effectively infinite retention because rotate-events.sh
is a stub that never deletes). The tier-2-to-tier-3 adapter (witness src/ingest_entire.py) was built
and tested June 8 against exactly the .entire/metadata/<sid>/full.jsonl path; only the trigger was
missing. Executed: daily scheduled task witness-transcript-ingest (06:36 local), idempotent, both
.entire roots, error write-back to the tracking signal.
upstream_control_path: /Users/brien/.claude/scheduled-tasks/witness-transcript-ingest/SKILL.md
catch_mechanism: task step 5 appends run failures to SIG-WITNESS-ENTIRE-ONGOING-INGEST-2026-06-08;
residual gap: scheduled tasks only run while the app is open, which ties this loop's reliability to
the 48-day-old always-on-hosting decision (register row 2). Follow-ups NOT built: store-append verb
for ingest_cc_transcript.py (CC-native transcripts currently read-only for friction analysis), and
git-extraction backfill of the 23 shadow-branch-only recoverable sessions.

## Liveness (design amended, build deferred to register row 10)

Opus falsified the ops-lease signal's "substrate is 80% built on Loom" instinct: Loom's live() window
is 120 seconds wide because nothing re-fires heartbeats mid-session (149 heartbeats across 1888
threads; sessions register, go dark, seal). Meanwhile entire status already delivers real liveness
(session id, prompt, start, last-activity, tokens; repo-scoped, which matches the per-repo lease
design), verified working, maintained upstream. Amended design recorded on the register: entire
status parsed defensively as the liveness read-path, one new Loom record kind carrying
op_class/target/ttl written at the two racing seams (suite runs, bulk writes), entire's activity
clock as the crash detector, no lease jsonl, no TTL sweeper. Stated dependency: a third-party CLI in
the coordination fabric, acceptable because it fails open to today's behavior.

## Delivery (executed today, the root cause)

The write side of cross-session handoff worked all along; there was no read side. Executed:
.context/PENDING_DECISIONS.md register (10 open Brien-gated decisions, oldest 51 days) plus
overwatch Section 14 on both surfaces (command file and Forge SKILL), which reads the register every
sweep, write-through reconciles it against signal dirs, and escalates any item surfaced twice
unanswered or older than 45 days above the fold.
upstream_control_path: /Users/brien/Workspaces/.claude/commands/overwatch.md Section 14 (mirror:
Core/products/forge/outputs/claude-code/meta/overwatch/SKILL.md Section 14)
catch_mechanism: Section 14 itself is the catch-net; register drift is detected by its freshness
cross-check step. Unproven until the next overwatch sweep actually runs it.

## Honest residuals

The loop is installed but not yet observed working end-to-end: no overwatch sweep has run Section 14,
the ingest task has not had its first run, and the liveness increment is deliberately unbuilt pending
Brien's row-10 call. Status stays captured until a sweep and a task run demonstrate the read side
live.

content.text:
**TL;DR:** The ladder paid for itself twice. First, your suspicion about working the logs outside Claude Code's governance turned out to be a loop some session already designed on June 8: the Witness adapter that reads Entire's transcript checkpoints was built, tested, and proven, and only the trigger was ever missing. That trigger now exists as a daily scheduled task. Second, Opus falsified your note B's core assumption: Loom is not 80% of a liveness channel, it is functionally blind (sessions show "live" for only 120 seconds after registering, because nothing ever re-fires a heartbeat, 149 heartbeats across 1888 threads), while entire status already delivers real liveness for free. **Status:** preservation and delivery are installed and committed; liveness is deliberately deferred to your call. **Your move:** one recommendation at the bottom.

**The synthesis, in your terms:** cross-session awareness decomposes into three primitives, preservation (can a past session's work be reached), liveness (can a running session be seen), and delivery (does a handoff arrive anywhere). All three existed in pieces. None were connected. And the reason none were connected is the third primitive itself: every prior session that discovered a piece filed an honest, durable handoff that nothing delivered. Five confirmed instances now, including your two pasted notes and the June 8 Witness design. The system's write side has been working all along. It never had a read side.

**What executed this turn (all L4, all committed):**
- **Delivery:** /Users/brien/Workspaces/.context/PENDING_DECISIONS.md, the register of all 10 open decisions gated on you (verified against 329 grep hits, 11 items confirmed already decided and excluded). Plus overwatch Section 14 on both surfaces, which reads it every sweep, reconciles it against the signal streams, and escalates anything surfaced twice unanswered or older than 45 days above the fold. Your "I can't tell if these are real" question becomes a report line instead of archaeology.
- **Preservation:** scheduled task witness-transcript-ingest, daily at 6:36am, running the already-built idempotent adapter against both .entire roots. Transcripts now land in Witness's append-only, queryable, Brien-governed store the day they're checkpointed, immune to the 7-day trim. Bonus finding: Witness's own rotation script is a stub that never deletes, so the store is already infinite-retention in practice.
- **Housekeeping from note B:** loom's .entire/ residue, finally gitignored and committed.

**What's deferred, honestly:** the liveness increment (entire status parsed as read-path, one Loom op-class record at the two racing seams, no lease jsonl, entire's activity clock replacing the TTL sweeper) is register row 10 and stays your call. The CC-native transcript store-append and the git-extraction backfill of the 23 shadow-branch-recoverable sessions are named follow-ups in the synthesis signal at /Users/brien/Workspaces/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-07-03-cross-session-awareness-ladder.md. And the installed loop is unproven until the next overwatch sweep and first ingest run actually fire, so its signal stays captured, not resolved.

**Recommendation:** decide register row 2 first, always-on-hosting, 48 days old. It is the root of the "reach" half of your goal, and it now also gates the new preservation loop, because scheduled tasks only run while the app is open. Every other row ages gracefully; that one silently caps everything built today. Unless you'd rather clear the oldest row first (the fieldbook $25 exception, 51 days, but low blast radius), row 2 is where I'd point you.

# Receipt-coverage audit + transaction-vs-receipt reconciliation + $25 no-receipt exception

## Observation

Three related gaps surfaced during April receipt chase:

### Gap 1: No coverage audit per travel day

The pipeline currently runs compliance checks PER TRANSACTION (R-MEAL-CAP, R-TIP-MAX, R-ALCOHOL, R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED). It does NOT check **whether each expected meal slot is covered** for a given travel day. Brien may have:
- Breakfast (paper receipt OR SUBAHQ default $5.94)
- Lunch (paper receipt OR SUBAHQ default $6.91)
- Dinner (paper receipt OR Caviar/DoorDash email)

If a slot is empty on a travel day, that's information — either Brien skipped that meal (legitimate) or forgot to capture (gap). The system should ASK rather than silently proceed.

### Gap 2: No reconciliation of card transactions vs receipts

Brien's primary signal of "did this expense happen" is his card statement, not the receipt. Receipts confirm details (vendor, line items, tip), but the existence of a charge is in the bank/card data. The pipeline currently has no card-statement input, so transactions without receipts are invisible.

If we ingested card statement (CSV from his bank or a manual import), the audit could:
- For each card line item in a travel-week date range with a Philadelphia/Camden vendor → expect a receipt
- For each receipt in the inbox → expect a matching card line
- Flag mismatches

### Gap 3: $25 no-receipt exception not encoded

Brien states he has an exception (verify with Time Approval) for items **under $25** to not require receipts. The current policy YAML has:

```yaml
R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED:
  low_risk_threshold_cents: 1000   # $10 — what's currently in policy
  corporate_card_exception_cents: 7500
```

Brien's claim: $2500 (\$25) for the consultant tier. **This needs verification** — if confirmed, the YAML threshold updates and the audit becomes much more permissive for breakfast/coffee/incidentals. Many of the SUBAHQ items, the small Lyft rides, and the coffee shops fall in the $10–$25 range.

## Required behavior
16:  Gap C ($25 no-receipt exception): PENDING — Brien to confirm threshold with Time Approval
21:     low_risk_verified_date when Brien confirms with Time Approval
22:remaining_work: "Brien confirms $25 exception with Time Approval; updates confidence_matrix.yml low_risk_verified_date"
24:# Receipt-coverage audit + transaction-vs-receipt reconciliation + $25 no-receipt exception
48:### Gap 3: $25 no-receipt exception not encoded
114:The audit treats items under $25 as `OK` (pass) when no receipt, with a `note: "low-risk, under-threshold, no receipt required"` instead of `WARN: missing receipt`.

=== always-on-hosting signal ===
---
id: SIG-037
timestamp: 2026-05-16T00:00:00Z
source: knowledge-ingest
confidence: 0.7
trust: 0.35
autonomy_level: L1
status: captured
cluster: deployment-topology
author: agent
related_intents: []
referenced_by:
  - THM-005
parent_signal: null
---

# "Laptop is not a strategy" — always-on hosting is the unresolved load-bearing decision

## Summary

The Rahul playbook (THM-005) independently states the exact failure Intent already names in its deployment-topology section: the processing pipeline must run somewhere always-on; "cron stops at 4am... nobody notices until Monday." Intent's CLAUDE.md explicitly flags this ("Brien's laptop goes offline during travel") and lists "Hosted deployment mode — always-on processing" as an open item. External corroboration raises the priority: every autonomous-execution claim Intent makes (L3/L4, autonomous pipeline) is undeliverable until this is resolved.

## Signal Value

This is not new information — it is *pressure on an existing unresolved decision*. The enrichment pipeline (Source → Dedup → Context → Trust Scorer → Classifier → Router) and any L3/L4 loop are vapor without a 24/7 substrate. The local-vs-always-on partition heuristic from the playbook (which stages run on-demand vs. must be always-on) is a usable organizing axis for the hosted-mode spec.

## Proposed Actions

1. Promote hosted-mode from open-item to a decision needing a DDR: GitHub Actions vs. cloud service vs. dedicated machine (options already enumerated in CLAUDE.md).
2. Use the local/always-on partition as the structuring axis: classify each pipeline stage by whether it can be on-demand.
3. Do NOT adopt the playbook's vendor-adjacent hosting recommendation or its cost math without independent sourcing.
</content>

DDR-007-ke-mcp-query-write-back.md

1	---
2	id: SIG-2026-05-28-orchestration-debt-work-queue-gap
3	created: 2026-05-28
4	type: architectural-debt
5	status: symptom-repaired, upstream-pending
6	severity: high
7	captured_by: brien-direct-observation
8	upstream_control_path: NONE TODAY — proposed write-through requires a new sibling product at the orchestration position (between Intent decisions and Cortège fetch fabric). Working name: `quartermaster` (orchestration substrate). This signal is the captured observation; the write-through is not yet built.
9	catch_mechanism: NONE TODAY — there is no chain_audit invariant for "every blocked task has a wake-on-unblock trigger" or "every Brien-action L0 item has a scheduled reminder" or "every L4-eligible item gets executed within session". Today's session manifested all three gaps. Proposed candidate invariants: I-BLOCKED-TASK-HAS-WAKE, I-L0-ACTION-HAS-REMINDER, I-L4-ITEM-NOT-SOFT-QUEUED.
10	pipeline_survival: yes — this signal persists; will surface in next overwatch sweep until upstream control lands.
11	related:
12	  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-library-7tier-execution-summary (parent — surfaced the manual-orchestration smell)
13	  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-library-7tier-continuation (parent — explicit "handoff" structure is itself a symptom)
14	  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-opus-repass-scheduled-task-misfire (sibling — scheduled task fired after work was done; no idempotency mechanism)
15	  - SIG-2026-05-28-closure-dod-bash-mutation-bypass (sibling — closure-DoD hook gap; same family of orchestration debt)
16	  - feedback_overwatch_orchestrate_not_execute (memory — Overwatch is dashboard + reminder, not orchestrator)
17	---
18	
19	# Orchestration debt — work-queue + scheduled-checkin gap surfaced during 7-tier Cast run
20	
21	## Observation (Brien-direct, 2026-05-28 ~22:50 ET)
22	
23	> "i feel like we skipped past some things we either previously built or intended to build in order to chain this all together. for example cortege was not properly functioning and only partial work was done. we need our work management queuing to be capable of holding the to do list and working through it and scheduling check ins etc to see if the blocked steps can kick off. i feel like we spent a whole week designing this and upgrading everything and now trying to do a refresh feels very manual"
24	
25	## What today's session manifested
26	
27	This session is the exemplar. Symptoms:
28	
29	1. **Cortège dispatcher exits after dispatch, not after fetch.** Workers are spawn-chips that need Brien-click to start. No automatic worker-start; no automatic re-dispatch when batch completes. The Round 2 of 73 URLs sits queued until a human triggers the next Dispatcher spawn.
30	2. **Worker session = chip in UI.** If Brien isn't at his desk with the right Claude Desktop session focusable, the chips can't be clicked. The orchestration is gated on UI navigation, not on signal-driven execution.
31	3. **Budget decisions are manual interview questions.** I surfaced "T2 freshening-delta would exceed 35% — your call." That's an L0 micro-decision per session. The system has no policy for "Brien pre-authorized class X spending up to Y; auto-execute below threshold."
32	4. **L0 reminders are written into freeform response text.** "Don't forget linkedin-capture tomorrow" lives in my reply — no scheduled wake-up, no calendar trigger, no surfacable-at-next-session anchor. Same for BC1-BC5 ("when you return").
33	5. **Blocked-task auto-resume is absent.** T2 SYNTHESIS is gated on Cortège harvest landing. Today, that gating is expressed in prose ("see continuation signal"). There is no `wake_when: corpus-fresh-for(slug)` predicate that auto-dispatches synthesis when its precondition resolves.
34	6. **Cross-session task persistence is partial.** In-session TaskList exists; cross-session task continuity is via signal files + Brien-memory. No durable queue with "blocked → waiting → ready → in-progress → done" state.
35	
36	## What we previously built or intended that would have helped
37	
38	- **Intent framework** — captures decisions and signals, but doesn't EXECUTE them as a work plan.
39	- **chain_audit invariants** — surface drift, but don't drive auto-fix.
40	- **Cortège** — fetch fabric, but its scope stops at fetch; downstream synthesis/wiring is unowned.
41	- **Overwatch** — dashboard + reminder, not orchestrator (per memory).
42	- **signal-stream** — observability stream with closure-DoD, but no scheduler.
43	- **schedule / loop / ScheduleWakeup** — discrete timers, not a unified planner.
44	- **Forge composition engine** — composes skills, but doesn't run a queue.
45	
46	## What's missing (proposed write-through — working name: `quartermaster`)
47	
48	A sibling product at the **orchestration position**:
49	
50	```
51	Plans / Decisions (Intent)          ← what to do, why
52	        ↓
53	Work Queue (quartermaster)          ← MISSING — when, by whom, with what budget, on what trigger
54	        ↓
55	Fetch Fabric (Cortège)              ← how to harvest
56	Synthesis (cast, voices, others)    ← how to think
57	Observability (signal-stream)       ← how we know it landed
58	```
59	
60	Core verbs (port contract — first draft):
61	- `enqueue(task, when: ready|blocked, deps: [...], budget: bucket, wake_on: [predicates])` — submit work with explicit blockers + wake predicates
62	- `tick()` — re-evaluate queue; promote blocked → ready when predicates resolve; dispatch ready tasks per budget policy
63	- `surface(role: brien|claude, channel: signal|chip|notification)` — escalate items that need human action
64	- `checkin(cron|condition)` — schedule periodic re-evaluation (replaces ad-hoc launchd plists)
65	- `budget_authorize(class, cap, period)` — Brien pre-authorizes budget classes; auto-decisions below cap
66	
67	Sibling-composability with existing products:
68	- Reads Intent decisions to know WHY a task exists (attribution chain)
69	- Subscribes to chain_audit invariant fires to auto-enqueue catch-net work
70	- Wraps Cortège for fetch tasks (Cortège becomes one of N workers)
71	- Writes signal-stream events for observability
72	
73	## Why "symptom-repaired, upstream-pending" today
74	
75	Today's symptom-fix:
76	- Manual interview rounds for decisions (worked but proved the gap)
77	- Manual budget threshold surfacing (worked but burns Brien attention)
78	- Gap-harvest agent dispatched as one-shot Sonnet job (correct execution; not durable mechanism)
79	- Cron plist scaffolded for metadata-freshening (per-decision launchd plist; not a planner)
80	
81	Upstream control would be: `quartermaster` product scaffolded with the port contract above + chain_audit invariants for the three L4-discipline gaps + Brien-policy file defining pre-authorized budget classes.
82	
83	## Concrete near-term remediation (this week)
84	
85	1. **Brainstorm session for `quartermaster`** with Brien (60-90min, the kind of session Throughline / coherence-engineering work has gotten). Output: WS-DDR draft + INTENT.md scaffold.
86	2. **Inventory existing one-shot artifacts** that should become quartermaster-managed tasks: scheduled launchd plists, recurring cron entries, ad-hoc spawn_task chips, in-flight continuation signals.
87	3. **Carve scope**: what's quartermaster vs. what's Forge vs. what's Cortège. Boundary discipline matters here (recent pattern: WS-DDR-025 sibling-over-parent-child).
88	4. **Stub the port verbs as a no-op API** — even a YAML-on-disk implementation would be enough to start moving manual work into it.
89	
90	## Closure DoD for this signal
91	
92	- [x] Observation captured verbatim (Brien quote preserved)
93	- [x] Six symptoms enumerated with today's session as exemplar
94	- [x] Existing products inventoried (what helps, what doesn't)
95	- [x] Write-through proposed (`quartermaster` product + 5 port verbs + 3 candidate chain_audit invariants)
96	- [x] Honesty mark: status is `symptom-repaired, upstream-pending` — not resolved
97	- [x] Near-term remediation steps enumerated
98	- [ ] Brainstorm session scheduled (Brien action — see L0-reminder below)
99	- [ ] First INTENT.md + WS-DDR draft (downstream of brainstorm)
100	- [ ] No catch-net invariant exists today; three proposed — will be installed once quartermaster has minimum surface
101	
102	## L0 reminders attached to this signal
103	
104	- **L0-2 reminder (tomorrow morning):** activate `linkedin-capture` skill so 4 LinkedIn-blocked tasks can drain
105	- **L0-3 reminder (when Brien returns):** BC1-BC5 decision interview from SIG-PCU-PHASE-3-UPLIFT — 5 architectural calls (Schema v2 fate, SQLite substrate, observability shape, contract artifact depth, sibling-spec ratification sequencing)
106	- **L0-4 reminder (this week):** schedule `quartermaster` brainstorm session
107

severity: high
captured_by: brien-direct-observation
upstream_control_path: NONE TODAY — proposed write-through requires a new sibling product at the orchestration position (between Intent decisions and Cortège fetch fabric). Working name: `quartermaster` (orchestration substrate). This signal is the captured observation; the write-through is not yet built.
catch_mechanism: NONE TODAY — there is no chain_audit invariant for "every blocked task has a wake-on-unblock trigger" or "every Brien-action L0 item has a scheduled reminder" or "every L4-eligible item gets executed within session". Today's session manifested all three gaps. Proposed candidate invariants: I-BLOCKED-TASK-HAS-WAKE, I-L0-ACTION-HAS-REMINDER, I-L4-ITEM-NOT-SOFT-QUEUED.
pipeline_survival: yes — this signal persists; will surface in next overwatch sweep until upstream control lands.
related:
  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-library-7tier-execution-summary (parent — surfaced the manual-orchestration smell)
  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-library-7tier-continuation (parent — explicit "handoff" structure is itself a symptom)
  - SIG-2026-05-28-cast-opus-repass-scheduled-task-misfire (sibling — scheduled task fired after work was done; no idempotency mechanism)
  - SIG-2026-05-28-closure-dod-bash-mutation-bypass (sibling — closure-DoD hook gap; same family of orchestration debt)
  - feedback_overwatch_orchestrate_not_execute (memory — Overwatch is dashboard + reminder, not orchestrator)
---

# Orchestration debt — work-queue + scheduled-checkin gap surfaced during 7-tier Cast run

## Observation (Brien-direct, 2026-05-28 ~22:50 ET)

> "i feel like we skipped past some things we either previously built or intended to build in order to chain this all together. for example cortege was not properly functioning and only partial work was done. we need our work management queuing to be capable of holding the to do list and working through it and scheduling check ins etc to see if the blocked steps can kick off. i feel like we spent a whole week designing this and upgrading everything and now trying to do a refresh feels very manual"

## What today's session manifested

This session is the exemplar. Symptoms:

1. **Cortège dispatcher exits after dispatch, not after fetch.** Workers are spawn-chips that need Brien-click to start. No automatic worker-start; no automatic re-dispatch when batch completes. The Round 2 of 73 URLs sits queued until a human triggers the next Dispatcher spawn.
2. **Worker session = chip in UI.** If Brien isn't at his desk with the right Claude Desktop session focusable, the chips can't be clicked. The orchestration is gated on UI navigation, not on signal-driven execution.
3. **Budget decisions are manual interview questions.** I surfaced "T2 freshening-delta would exceed 35% — your call." That's an L0 micro-decision per session. The system has no policy for "Brien pre-authorized class X spending up to Y; auto-execute below threshold."
4. **L0 reminders are written into freeform response text.** "Don't forget linkedin-capture tomorrow" lives in my reply — no scheduled wake-up, no calendar trigger, no surfacable-at-next-session anchor. Same for BC1-BC5 ("when you return").
5. **Blocked-task auto-resume is absent.** T2 SYNTHESIS is gated on Cortège harvest landing. Today, that gating is expressed in prose ("see continuation signal"). There is no `wake_when: corpus-fresh-for(slug)` predicate that auto-dispatches synthesis when its precondition resolves.
6. **Cross-session task persistence is partial.** In-session TaskList exists; cross-session task continuity is via signal files + Brien-memory. No durable queue with "blocked → waiting → ready → in-progress → done" state.

## What we previously built or intended that would have helped

- **Intent framework** — captures decisions and signals, but doesn't EXECUTE them as a work plan.
- **chain_audit invariants** — surface drift, but don't drive auto-fix.
- **Cortège** — fetch fabric, but its scope stops at fetch; downstream synthesis/wiring is unowned.
- **Overwatch** — dashboard + reminder, not orchestrator (per memory).
- **signal-stream** — observability stream with closure-DoD, but no scheduler.
- **schedule / loop / ScheduleWakeup** — discrete timers, not a unified planner.
- **Forge composition engine** — composes skills, but doesn't run a queue.

## What's missing (proposed write-through — working name: `quartermaster`)

A sibling product at the **orchestration position**:

```
Plans / Decisions (Intent)          ← what to do, why
        ↓
Work Queue (quartermaster)          ← MISSING — when, by whom, with what budget, on what trigger
        ↓
Fetch Fabric (Cortège)              ← how to harvest
Synthesis (cast, voices, others)    ← how to think
Observability (signal-stream)       ← how we know it landed
```

Core verbs (port contract — first draft):
- `enqueue(task, when: ready|blocked, deps: [...], budget: bucket, wake_on: [predicates])` — submit work with explicit blockers + wake predicates
- `tick()` — re-evaluate queue; promote blocked → ready when predicates resolve; dispatch ready tasks per budget policy
- `surface(role: brien|claude, channel: signal|chip|notification)` — escalate items that need human action
- `checkin(cron|condition)` — schedule periodic re-evaluation (replaces ad-hoc launchd plists)
- `budget_authorize(class, cap, period)` — Brien pre-authorizes budget classes; auto-decisions below cap

Sibling-composability with existing products:
- Reads Intent decisions to know WHY a task exists (attribution chain)
- Subscribes to chain_audit invariant fires to auto-enqueue catch-net work
- Wraps Cortège for fetch tasks (Cortège becomes one of N workers)
- Writes signal-stream events for observability

## Why "symptom-repaired, upstream-pending" today

Today's symptom-fix:
- Manual interview rounds for decisions (worked but proved the gap)
- Manual budget threshold surfacing (worked but burns Brien attention)
- Gap-harvest agent dispatched as one-shot Sonnet job (correct execution; not durable mechanism)
- Cron plist scaffolded for metadata-freshening (per-decision launchd plist; not a planner)

Upstream control would be: `quartermaster` product scaffolded with the port contract above + chain_audit invariants for the three L4-discipline gaps + Brien-policy file defining pre-authorized budget classes.

## Concrete near-term remediation (this week)

1. **Brainstorm session for `quartermaster`** with Brien (60-90min, the kind of session Throughline / coherence-engineering work has gotten). Output: WS-DDR draft + INTENT.md scaffold.
2. **Inventory existing one-shot artifacts** that should become quartermaster-managed tasks: scheduled launchd plists, recurring cron entries, ad-hoc spawn_task chips, in-flight continuation signals.
3. **Carve scope**: what's quartermaster vs. what's Forge vs. what's Cortège. Boundary discipline matters here (recent pattern: WS-DDR-025 sibling-over-parent-child).
4. **Stub the port verbs as a no-op API** — even a YAML-on-disk implementation would be enough to start moving manual work into it.

## Closure DoD for this signal

- [x] Observation captured verbatim (Brien quote preserved)
- [x] Six symptoms enumerated with today's session as exemplar
- [x] Existing products inventoried (what helps, what doesn't)
- [x] Write-through proposed (`quartermaster` product + 5 port verbs + 3 candidate chain_audit invariants)
- [x] Honesty mark: status is `symptom-repaired, upstream-pending` — not resolved
- [x] Near-term remediation steps enumerated
- [ ] Brainstorm session scheduled (Brien action — see L0-reminder below)
- [ ] First INTENT.md + WS-DDR draft (downstream of brainstorm)
- [ ] No catch-net invariant exists today; three proposed — will be installed once quartermaster has minimum surface

## L0 reminders attached to this signal

- **L0-2 reminder (tomorrow morning):** activate `linkedin-capture` skill so 4 LinkedIn-blocked tasks can drain
- **L0-3 reminder (when Brien returns):** BC1-BC5 decision interview from SIG-PCU-PHASE-3-UPLIFT — 5 architectural calls (Schema v2 fate, SQLite substrate, observability shape, contract artifact depth, sibling-spec ratification sequencing)
- **L0-4 reminder (this week):** schedule `quartermaster` brainstorm session
