content.content:
# Fieldbook — Expense Lifecycle System

## Overview

Composable expense lifecycle system for traveling consultants. Core pipeline implemented and
unit-tested (v0.3.0, 736 passed, 1 xfailed as of 2026-07-01 evening; see the Tests section below for
the live count); the single operator entrypoint (`update-expenses`) landed 2026-07-01 per
`spec/2026-07-01-fieldbook-review-and-redesign-decision.md`. Listener + real-OCR completion in
progress (see `spec/2026-06-01-listener-ocr-completion-plan.md`). Tier / verification status:
`.intent/INTENT.md` and `Core/PRODUCT-ACCOUNTABILITY-LEDGER.md`.

- **Pipeline:** INTAKE → LEDGER → COMPLY → NARRATE → EXPORT
- **Users:** Brien (1099 via Turnberry/Subaru) and Dean Gabbert (W-2 FTE via Turnberry/ASA)
- **Design spec:** `spec/fieldbook-design.md`
- **Intent model:** `.intent/INTENT.md`, `.intent/decisions.md`
- **Engagement data:** `Work/Consulting/Engagements/[Client]/financials/` (per-client, not here)

## Pipeline Sequence

| Stage | Module | Role |
|-------|--------|------|
| INTAKE | `src/intake.py` | Inbox scan, receipt classification, filing |
| LEDGER | `src/ledger.py` | Append-only source of truth, engagement-scoped |
| COMPLY | `src/compliance.py` | Per-transaction multi-flag policy audit with version + cite |
| NARRATE | `src/narrative.py` | True Cost of Travel model, persona-dependent (Brien 1099 vs Dean W-2) |
| EXPORT | `src/export.py` | Projector PSA workbook generator via openpyxl |

Support modules: `src/styles.py` (openpyxl styles), `src/profile.py` (user config),
`src/profile_runner.py` + `src/baseline_runner.py` (end-to-end runners),
`src/matchers/` (classification matchers).

## Invocation

**Corrected 2026-07-01 (Action 8, per `spec/2026-07-01-fieldbook-review-and-redesign-decision.md`
policy P-A).** The five module commands formerly listed here (`src/intake.py`, `src/ledger.py`,
`src/compliance.py`, `src/narrative.py`, `src/export.py`) have no `__main__` block and were
always silent no-ops (running them did nothing). Those five modules (plus `src/narrative_fte.py`)
are pure function libraries, never run standalone. Of them, `ledger.py`, `compliance.py`, and
`export.py` are called by `src/orchestrator.py`; `intake.py` is imported only by `src/ocr.py`
and the listeners; `narrative.py` and `narrative_fte.py` currently have no non-test caller at
all (the `narrative_model` dispatcher is parked per the decision doc, section 4, "Deliberately
NOT this wave").
Everything documented below is a command that was executed successfully during this correction
pass, no fiction.

### Venv (required for openpyxl + pytest)

Venv lives at `Core/products/fieldbook/.venv/`. Use the venv python directly — no activation needed.

```bash
# First-time setup
cd /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/fieldbook
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

### The one operator command: update-expenses

The real operator surface, per policy P-A, is a single installed console command
(`[project.scripts]` in pyproject.toml, module at `scripts/update_expenses.py`). It chains
window resolution -> confirm gate -> ledger seed -> pipeline -> code-owned state writes
(`.last-run.yml` v2, run manifest, closure signal) in one invocation. Verified flags
(`update-expenses --help`, run 2026-07-01):

```bash
.venv/bin/update-expenses \
  --profile <engagement>/financials/fieldbook-profile.yml \
  --input-csv <expenses>/<batch>/<batch>_input.csv \
  --receipts-dir <expenses>/<batch>/receipts \
  --batch <submission-batch-id> \
  (--engagement-dir <engagement-root> | --state-dir <flat-state-dir>) \
  [--output-dir <dir>] [--purpose <slug>] \
  [--signals-dir <dir>] \
  [--date-from YYYY-MM-DD] [--date-to YYYY-MM-DD] \
  [--yes] [--full] [--travel-schedule PATH] [--allow-no-receipts]
```

**Default output routing (added 2026-07-02, layout-wiring pass; contract
CON-FIELDBOOK-LAYOUT-EXPENSES-001).** `--output-dir` is now OPTIONAL. When omitted, the run
lands under `<batch-folder>/replays/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>/`, where the batch folder is the
directory holding `--input-csv` and the slug is `--purpose` (default `run`); a same-day slug
collision gets a numeric suffix so a rerun never clobbers an earlier run. This puts replay /
verification reruns inside the batch folder per the contract (section 5.1), never at the batch
root, with no path construction by the operator. An explicit `--output-dir` is honored
unchanged (backward compatible). The run manifest and worklist always follow the resolved
output dir.

Since Action 5 (D-015, 2026-07-01) the engagement has ONE spine ledger (`ledger.xlsx` beside
`.last-run.yml`); `--batch` is required, seeding is idempotent (append new / supersede changed /
never wipe), report artifacts are window+batch projections, and a (date, amount, vendor) triple
claimed in two batches fails the build with exit 6. Ledger corrections that must survive
rebuilds go into the batch input CSV (or a re-batch); ledger-side edits via `fieldbook-ledger`
(add-entry / adjust-entry / supersede, see `--help`) are superseded back to CSV content on the
next seed of that batch, with the audit chain preserved. Authoritative submitted artifacts
resolve through `submissions.yml` (the registry; `reconcile`/`sync` consume it).

`--engagement-dir` and `--state-dir` are mutually exclusive. `--full` runs
`orchestrator.run_full_process` instead of `run_pipeline`; pass `--travel-schedule PATH`
(format: `docs/contracts/travel-schedule-contract.md`) to arm the travel calendar and get
real per-night hotel-coverage gating (Action 4, landed 2026-07-01); without a schedule the
command still warns that coverage is vacuous. `--allow-no-receipts` is required
today because the seeded ledger's `receipt_ref` is always empty (Action 7 territory). Exit
codes: 0 success, 2 first-run window-guess refusal, 3 confirm-gate refusal, 4 receipt-gate
refusal, 5 corrupt/unmigrated state (fail closed), 6 cross-batch exclusivity violation. Full stage-by-stage walkthrough and MANUAL
stage markers: `skills/financials/update-expenses/SKILL.md`.

### The layout catch mechanism: fieldbook-layout-audit

Added 2026-07-02 (layout-wiring pass). A read-only console command
(`[project.scripts]`, module at `src/layout_audit.py`) that validates an engagement
`financials/expenses/` tree against the folder-layout contract
(CON-FIELDBOOK-LAYOUT-EXPENSES-001): required root spine files, single-inbox rule (staging/,
grandfathered receipts-inbox/), unknown-at-root detection, batch-folder shape (no per-batch
ledger.xlsx unless a MIGRATED-README.md grandfathers it; replays/ tolerated), and registry
pointer resolution. It moves and edits NOTHING.

```bash
.venv/bin/fieldbook-layout-audit <engagement>/financials/expenses
# exit 0 clean / 1 findings / 2 broken registry pointer
```

Live Subaru run 2026-07-02: exit 1, 5 findings, 0 broken pointers (4 retention-gated
`.bak-2026-07-01` files awaiting their 2026-07-31 archive window, plus `CLEANUP-PLAN` and a
`ride_report_2026_03_08.csv` stray at root). These are FINDINGS to triage, not test failures;
the registry resolves cleanly.

### The intake sweep gate: fieldbook-intake

`fieldbook-intake --staging-dir <dir> --receipts-dir <dir>` sweeps a staging dir (e.g.
~/Downloads) into the engagement receipts dir. Since 2026-07-02 it applies an
**expense-likeness gate** (contract 7.1 / D-4): a file is filed only when its name is already
in the receipt convention, OR a valid vision sidecar sits beside it, OR extraction recovers a
non-garbled amount or date. Everything else is REPORTED as residue (path + reason) and LEFT in
place, never filed. This closes the over-reach that filed 15 non-expense files from a
Downloads-root sweep. The photo_ocr worklist is now merge-by-id across runs (FB-INTAKE-1
truncation fix), and residue is persisted to the worklist `residue:` block so a drop zone never
silently keeps.

### Real CLI mains (verified with --help/-h or direct invocation, 2026-07-01)

Five modules have genuine `argparse`-based (or bare-positional) CLIs with a `__main__` block.
Documented nowhere prior to this correction. All invoked from the repo root with the venv python.

```bash
# expense_coverage (audit ledger coverage over a date range; positional: ledger path)
.venv/bin/python src/expense_coverage.py --from 2026-06-01 --to 2026-06-30 <path-to-ledger.xlsx>

# reconcile (reconcile a card/bank statement CSV against the ledger)
.venv/bin/python src/reconcile.py --statement <csv-path>:<schema>:<card-last4> <path-to-ledger.xlsx>
# --statement is repeatable; --from/--to optional

# sync (reconcile Harvest expenses vs the ledger; positional: engagement label, ledger path)
.venv/bin/python src/sync.py <engagement-label> <path-to-ledger.xlsx> [--from ISO] [--to ISO]

# ocr (extract receipt fields from an image or PDF; bare positional path, no flags)
.venv/bin/python src/ocr.py <receipt-path>
# zero args: prints available OCR engines plus a usage line, exit 0

# ocr_extract (parse already-OCR'd receipt text into structured fields)
.venv/bin/python src/ocr_extract.py <text-file-path>   # or pipe text via stdin
```

`expense_coverage.py`, `reconcile.py`, and `sync.py` are standard argparse and respond to
`--help` normally. `ocr.py` and `ocr_extract.py` have no argparse layer: their first
positional argument is read as a literal file path, so `--help` is treated as a (nonexistent)
filename. Verified 2026-07-01: `ocr_extract.py --help` errors with a `FileNotFoundError`
traceback (exit 1), while `ocr.py --help` degrades gracefully, emitting a JSON result with
`"confidence": "unverifiable"` and a `file-not-found` verify flag at exit 0. Use a real path
or, for `ocr.py`, no argument at all.

### Dev-diagnostic runners (not general operator commands)

`src/baseline_runner.py` and `src/profile_runner.py` take no CLI arguments: they read
hardcoded module-level path constants pointing at one specific historical Subaru baseline
capture (`ENGAGEMENT_DIR = Path("/Users/brien/Workspaces/Work/Consulting/Engagements/Subaru/
financials")`, `baseline_runner.py:22`). They predate the profile model and exist to
establish/diff a known-good baseline; they are not a general operator surface and will
`FileNotFoundError` against any other engagement or a moved input file (as observed
2026-07-01: `SOURCE_CSV` no longer exists at its hardcoded path). The canonical seeding
code now lives in `src/seeding.py` with `engagement` as a required parameter (Action 8
code half, D-014, landed 2026-07-01); both runners are thin importers with back-compat
re-exports and pass `engagement="Subaru"` explicitly. `scripts/import_march_2026.py` is a similar one-shot: a single
positional output-ledger-path argument, written to import one historical month's data once;
not a recurring command.

### Tests

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest -q                             # all tests (736 passed, 1 xfailed as of 2026-07-01 evening)
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_ledger.py           # single module
.venv/bin/pytest --cov=src                      # with coverage
.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_golden_eval.py   # golden-master eval gate (must stay green)
```

Test files (verified `ls tests/`, 2026-07-01): `test_compliance.py`, `test_coverage_timestamp.py`,
`test_cross_report_invariants.py`, `test_expense_coverage.py`, `test_export.py`,
`test_export_signing.py`, `test_gap_fill.py`, `test_golden_eval.py`, `test_intake.py`,
`test_intake_sanitize.py`, `test_integration.py`, `test_ledger.py`, `test_listener_apple_mail.py`,
`test_listener_gmail.py`, `test_listener_harvest.py`, `test_listeners_base.py`, `test_matchers.py`,
`test_meal_cap.py`, `test_narrative.py`, `test_narrative_fte.py`, `test_no_coverage_shadow.py`,
`test_ocr.py`, `test_ocr_extract.py`, `test_orchestrator.py`, `test_orchestrator_full.py`,
`test_profile.py`, `test_reconcile.py`, `test_scope.py`, `test_security_enforcement.py`,
`test_sync.py`, `test_trace_emit.py`, `test_travel.py`, `test_update_expenses.py`,
`test_verification_fixes.py`.

## Spec

Full design specification: `spec/fieldbook-design.md`
Covers: problem statement, users (Brien + Dean), architecture diagram, pipeline stage detail,
engagement data layout, and policy reference design.

## Intent model

- `Core/products/fieldbook/.intent/INTENT.md` — product intent, hypothesis, success criteria
- `Core/products/fieldbook/.intent/decisions.md` — 24 signals + decision log

## Related

- Global CLAUDE.md `## Fieldbook` section — project status, skills category, design spec pointer
- Forge skills category: `Core/products/forge/outputs/claude-code/financials/` — receipt-intake,
  expense-ledger, expense-compliance, expense-export, policy-reference, true-cost-of-travel,
  expense-narrative  # CONFIRMED: mirrored from fieldbook/skills/financials/ on 2026-05-20
  # Ownership: Fieldbook is source-of-truth; Forge mirrors for Claude-Code surface.
  # Sync: manual write-through when SKILL.md changes; automation pending Conduit.

(add-entry / adjust-entry / supersede, see `--help`) are superseded back to CSV content on the
next seed of that batch, with the audit chain preserved. Authoritative submitted artifacts
resolve through `submissions.yml` (the registry; `reconcile`/`sync` consume it).

`--engagement-dir` and `--state-dir` are mutually exclusive. `--full` runs
`orchestrator.run_full_process` instead of `run_pipeline`; pass `--travel-schedule PATH`
(format: `docs/contracts/travel-schedule-contract.md`) to arm the travel calendar and get
real per-night hotel-coverage gating (Action 4, landed 2026-07-01); without a schedule the
command still warns that coverage is vacuous. `--allow-no-receipts` is required
today because the seeded ledger's `receipt_ref` is always empty (Action 7 territory). Exit
codes: 0 success, 2 first-run window-guess refusal, 3 confirm-gate refusal, 4 receipt-gate
refusal, 5 corrupt/unmigrated state (fail closed), 6 cross-batch exclusivity violation. Full stage-by-stage walkthrough and MANUAL
stage markers: `skills/financials/update-expenses/SKILL.md`.

### The layout catch mechanism: fieldbook-layout-audit

Added 2026-07-02 (layout-wiring pass). A read-only console command
(`[project.scripts]`, module at `src/layout_audit.py`) that validates an engagement
`financials/expenses/` tree against the folder-layout contract
(CON-FIELDBOOK-LAYOUT-EXPENSES-001): required root spine files, single-inbox rule (staging/,
grandfathered receipts-inbox/), unknown-at-root detection, batch-folder shape (no per-batch
ledger.xlsx unless a MIGRATED-README.md grandfathers it; replays/ tolerated), and registry
pointer resolution. It moves and edits NOTHING.

```bash
.venv/bin/fieldbook-layout-audit <engagement>/financials/expenses
# exit 0 clean / 1 findings / 2 broken registry pointer
```

Live Subaru run 2026-07-02: exit 1, 5 findings, 0 broken pointers (4 retention-gated
`.bak-2026-07-01` files awaiting their 2026-07-31 archive window, plus `CLEANUP-PLAN` and a
`ride_report_2026_03_08.csv` stray at root). These are FINDINGS to triage, not test failures;
the registry resolves cleanly.

### The intake sweep gate: fieldbook-intake

`fieldbook-intake --staging-dir <dir> --receipts-dir <dir>` sweeps a staging dir (e.g.
~/Downloads) into the engagement receipts dir. Since 2026-07-02 it applies an
**expense-likeness gate** (contract 7.1 / D-4): a file is filed only when its name is already
in the receipt convention, OR a valid vision sidecar sits beside it, OR extraction recovers a
non-garbled amount or date. Everything else is REPORTED as residue (path + reason) and LEFT in
place, never filed. This closes the over-reach that filed 15 non-expense files from a
Downloads-root sweep. The photo_ocr worklist is now merge-by-id across runs (FB-INTAKE-1
truncation fix), and residue is persisted to the worklist `residue:` block so a drop zone never
silently keeps.

### Real CLI mains (verified with --help/-h or direct invocation, 2026-07-01)

Five modules have genuine `argparse`-based (or bare-positional) CLIs with a `__main__` block.
Documented nowhere prior to this correction. All invoked from the repo root with the venv python.

```bash
# expense_coverage (audit ledger coverage over a date range; positional: ledger path)
.venv/bin/python src/expense_coverage.py --from 2026-06-01 --to 2026-06-30 <path-to-ledger.xlsx>

# reconcile (reconcile a card/bank statement CSV against the ledger)
.venv/bin/python src/reconcile.py --statement <csv-path>:<schema>:<card-last4> <path-to-ledger.xlsx>
# --statement is repeatable; --from/--to optional

# sync (reconcile Harvest expenses vs the ledger; positional: engagement label, ledger path)
.venv/bin/python src/sync.py <engagement-label> <path-to-ledger.xlsx> [--from ISO] [--to ISO]

# ocr (extract receipt fields from an image or PDF; bare positional path, no flags)
.venv/bin/python src/ocr.py <receipt-path>
# zero args: prints available OCR engines plus a usage line, exit 0

# ocr_extract (parse already-OCR'd receipt text into structured fields)
.venv/bin/python src/ocr_extract.py <text-file-path>   # or pipe text via stdin
```

`expense_coverage.py`, `reconcile.py`, and `sync.py` are standard argparse and respond to
`--help` normally. `ocr.py` and `ocr_extract.py` have no argparse layer: their first
positional argument is read as a literal file path, so `--help` is treated as a (nonexistent)
filename. Verified 2026-07-01: `ocr_extract.py --help` errors with a `FileNotFoundError`
traceback (exit 1), while `ocr.py --help` degrades gracefully, emitting a JSON result with
`"confidence": "unverifiable"` and a `file-not-found` verify flag at exit 0. Use a real path
or, for `ocr.py`, no argument at all.

### Dev-diagnostic runners (not general operator commands)

`src/baseline_runner.py` and `src/profile_runner.py` take no CLI arguments: they read
hardcoded module-level path constants pointing at one specific historical Subaru baseline
capture (`ENGAGEMENT_DIR = Path("/Users/brien/Workspaces/Work/Consulting/Engagements/Subaru/
financials")`, `baseline_runner.py:22`). They predate the profile model and exist to
establish/diff a known-good baseline; they are not a general operator surface and will
`FileNotFoundError` against any other engagement or a moved input file (as observed
2026-07-01: `SOURCE_CSV` no longer exists at its hardcoded path). The canonical seeding
code now lives in `src/seeding.py` with `engagement` as a required parameter (Action 8
code half, D-014, landed 2026-07-01); both runners are thin importers with back-compat
re-exports and pass `engagement="Subaru"` explicitly. `scripts/import_march_2026.py` is a similar one-shot: a single
positional output-ledger-path argument, written to import one historical month's data once;
not a recurring command.

### Tests

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest -q                             # all tests (736 passed, 1 xfailed as of 2026-07-01 evening)
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_ledger.py           # single module
.venv/bin/pytest --cov=src                      # with coverage
.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_golden_eval.py   # golden-master eval gate (must stay green)
```

# Receipt-coverage audit + transaction-vs-receipt reconciliation + $25 no-receipt exception

## Observation

Three related gaps surfaced during April receipt chase:

### Gap 1: No coverage audit per travel day

The pipeline currently runs compliance checks PER TRANSACTION (R-MEAL-CAP, R-TIP-MAX, R-ALCOHOL, R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED). It does NOT check **whether each expected meal slot is covered** for a given travel day. Brien may have:
- Breakfast (paper receipt OR SUBAHQ default $5.94)
- Lunch (paper receipt OR SUBAHQ default $6.91)
- Dinner (paper receipt OR Caviar/DoorDash email)

If a slot is empty on a travel day, that's information — either Brien skipped that meal (legitimate) or forgot to capture (gap). The system should ASK rather than silently proceed.

### Gap 2: No reconciliation of card transactions vs receipts

Brien's primary signal of "did this expense happen" is his card statement, not the receipt. Receipts confirm details (vendor, line items, tip), but the existence of a charge is in the bank/card data. The pipeline currently has no card-statement input, so transactions without receipts are invisible.

If we ingested card statement (CSV from his bank or a manual import), the audit could:
- For each card line item in a travel-week date range with a Philadelphia/Camden vendor → expect a receipt
- For each receipt in the inbox → expect a matching card line
- Flag mismatches

### Gap 3: $25 no-receipt exception not encoded

Brien states he has an exception (verify with Time Approval) for items **under $25** to not require receipts. The current policy YAML has:

```yaml
R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED:
  low_risk_threshold_cents: 1000   # $10 — what's currently in policy
  corporate_card_exception_cents: 7500
```

Brien's claim: $2500 (\$25) for the consultant tier. **This needs verification** — if confirmed, the YAML threshold updates and the audit becomes much more permissive for breakfast/coffee/incidentals. Many of the SUBAHQ items, the small Lyft rides, and the coffee shops fall in the $10–$25 range.

## Required behavior

### A. Coverage audit module (`src/coverage.py`)

Reads the engagement profile `travel_pattern` block to know expected travel days, then for each travel day in the ledger checks:

```yaml
expected_slots_per_travel_day:
  - breakfast (pre_10am)
  - lunch (pre_2pm)
  - dinner (pre_10pm)
  - ground_transport (Lyft/Uber/cab)  # for arrival/departure days
  - lodging                            # for nights between
  - airfare                            # for arrival/departure days
```

For each slot, the audit reports:
- `COVERED` — receipt OR default-pattern entry exists
- `DEFAULT_USED` — fallback default applied (SUBAHQ pattern)
- `GAP_OK` — slot empty but consultant likely skipped (e.g., Friday home-bound)
- `GAP_FLAG` — slot empty and shouldn't be (e.g., dinner on a 3-hr-delayed travel-back day) → ask consultant

### B. Card-statement reconciliation (`src/reconcile.py`)

New profile field `card_statements`:

```yaml
card_statements:
  - card_last4: "5886"
    statement_glob: "Work/.../financials/cards/visa-5886-*.csv"
    schema: "chase-csv-v1"
  - card_last4: "8886"
    statement_glob: "Work/.../financials/cards/visa-8886-*.csv"
    schema: "amex-csv-v1"
```

The reconcile module does a 2-way match:
- Card line → receipt (by date + amount within tolerance ± $0.50)
- Receipt → card line
- Outputs a "transactions-without-receipts" list and "receipts-without-transactions" list (the latter often = mis-categorized personal cards)

This consumes statement CSVs but never the credentials — Brien exports manually until a feed integration exists.

### C. Update R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED threshold (PENDING VERIFICATION)

If Brien confirms the $25 exception with Time Approval:

```yaml
R-RECEIPT-REQUIRED:
  low_risk_threshold_cents: 2500   # was 1000, raised per consultant exception
  consultant_exception_note: "Verified with Time Approval YYYY-MM-DD by Brien Tate"
  exception_documentation_required: true   # flag in audit, not block submission
```

The audit treats items under $25 as `OK` (pass) when no receipt, with a `note: "low-risk, under-threshold, no receipt required"` instead of `WARN: missing receipt`.

## Connection to existing decisions

- **2026-04-01 — Confidence scoring at intake, not pipeline** (already filed): coverage audit applies AT REVIEW (post-intake), but uses confidence flags from intake to decide what to escalate.
- **D-011 Three-axis profile model**: travel_pattern + business_locations (SIG-015) + card_statements (this signal) → unified profile schema v0.3.
- **SIG-015 — Auto-classify business vs personal**: uses card_last4 as a signal. This signal extends to use card data for *coverage*, not just classification.

## Workaround until shipped

Manual coverage walk per travel week, using the gap-flag boxes in `april-receipts-download-checklist.html`. For each travel day:
1. Look at HEIC + Gmail-PDF inventory
2. Confirm breakfast + lunch + dinner + transport slots
3. For empty slots, recall whether you skipped or forgot
4. Apply the $25 exception manually for forgotten items under threshold

## Priority

**HIGH** — this gap is what made the April submission take 30+ min of manual chase instead of 5 min of audit-then-submit. Recurring monthly cost.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Confidence matrix — source quality signals → confidence score → verify flags.
#
# Per design spec §3.3 and §5.1–§5.2.
# Each source type maps to a base confidence score and a set of verify flags
# that gate ledger auto-commit. Intake.py reads this rather than hardcoded thresholds.
#
# Verify flag semantics:
#   verify:total    — total amount suspect; human must confirm
#   verify:tip      — tip line present but unclear; human must confirm
#   verify:date     — date unreadable or inconsistent with travel calendar
#   verify:vendor   — vendor name unclear or mis-classified
#   verify:split    — multi-person receipt; split needed
#   verify:category — category classification uncertain

sources:
  - type: digital_receipt_pdf
    description: "PDF from email (Caviar, Lyft, Amtrak, hotel folio)"
    confidence: 0.95
    verify_flags: []
    notes: Highest fidelity — machine-generated, direct from vendor system

  - type: email_receipt_html
    description: "HTML email body extracted via MCP (e.g., DoorDash, Grubhub)"
    confidence: 0.90
    verify_flags: []
    notes: "High fidelity when parsed cleanly; confidence drops to 0.70 if HTML-only
           and plaintext extraction failed (set html_extraction_failed: true)"

  - type: email_receipt_html_extraction_failed
    description: "HTML email where plaintext fallback was used (MCP body-only mode)"
    confidence: 0.70
    verify_flags: [verify:total, verify:vendor]
    notes: Applies when Cowork connector returns metadata-only; SIG-017 pattern

  - type: screenshot
    description: "Screenshot of receipt (HEIC, PNG, JPEG from iPhone Camera)"
    confidence: 0.75
    verify_flags: [verify:total, verify:vendor]
    notes: OCR quality depends on lighting and angle; tip lines especially unreliable

  - type: screenshot_crumpled
    description: "Photo of crumpled/folded paper receipt"
    confidence: 0.45
    verify_flags: [verify:tip, verify:total, verify:date, verify:vendor]
    notes: Worst-case optical quality; flag everything; human review mandatory

  - type: credit_card_statement_line
    description: "Line item from card statement CSV (no receipt)"
    confidence: 0.60
    verify_flags: [verify:tip, verify:total]
    notes: Amount is accurate (card-sourced) but tip and sub-totals absent

  - type: default_pattern
    description: "SUBAHQ system default (e.g., $5.94 breakfast / $6.91 lunch)"
    confidence: 0.85
    verify_flags: []
    notes: Vendor-established pattern; no receipt needed; high confidence by design

  - type: memory_entry
    description: "Brien-asserted entry with no receipt or card evidence"
    confidence: 0.35
    verify_flags: [verify:total, verify:vendor, verify:date, verify:category]
    notes: Manual assertion only; below auto-commit threshold; always human-reviewed

# Outcome-derived confidence adjustment (Action 3, 2026-07-01)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# The source-type confidences above are BASE values that assume the extraction
# actually worked. That assumption produced the confidence lie fixed on
# 2026-07-01 (spec/2026-07-01-fieldbook-review-and-redesign-decision.md,
# section 4 row 3): a real Element hotel folio read through the pure-Python
# PDF fallback yielded date=None / amount_cents=None yet still carried the
# digital_receipt_pdf 0.95 with zero verify flags, sailing over auto_commit.
#
# src/ocr.py now adjusts the base confidence by the extraction OUTCOME:
#   - total (amount_cents) missing: subtract missing_total_penalty, cap at
#     cap_when_total_missing (below auto_commit), and add verify:total.
#   - date missing: add verify:date (needs_verify signal). No numeric penalty:
#     intake backfills the date from the MMDD filename convention, and the
#     flag still gates auto-commit review (pinned by tests/test_intake.py).
#   - BOTH total and date missing: cap at cap_when_all_missing, inside the
#     reject band, so a parse that produced nothing can never auto-commit
#     regardless of source_type.
#   - pure-python-fallback PDF engine: cap at fallback_engine_cap and add
#     pdf-engine-fallback. The last-resort reader is demoted; only a real PDF
#     library (pdfplumber primary) can earn auto-commit confidence.
# src/ocr.py carries the same values as hardcoded defaults so a missing or
# malformed config file fails CLOSED (adjustments still apply), never back
# to the pre-2026-07-01 lie.
outcome_adjustments:
  missing_total_penalty: 0.20
  cap_when_total_missing: 0.70   # below auto_commit 0.80
  cap_when_all_missing: 0.30     # below reject 0.40: quarantine
  fallback_engine_cap: 0.70      # demoted pure-Python PDF reader ceiling
  min_confidence: 0.05

# Garble gate (Action 3, 2026-07-01)
# ----------------------------------
# src/ocr.py:_extract_pdf computes a token trust ratio over extracted PDF text
# (currency tokens, date/number tokens, and dictionary-plausible words vs all
# tokens). Below min_trust_ratio the extraction is UNTRUSTED: the result is
# confidence 'unverifiable' with a pdf-text-garbled flag and no parsed fields.
# Calibration on the real Element folio: clean pdfplumber text scores ~0.89,
# the CID-shifted fallback garble scores 0.0 (fixtures in tests/fixtures/ocr/).
garble_gate:
  min_trust_ratio: 0.35

# Commit thresholds
thresholds:
  auto_commit: 0.80       # confidence >= 0.80: auto-commit to ledger
  flag_for_review: 0.60   # 0.60 <= confidence < 0.80: commit + flag VERIFY_REQUIRED
  reject: 0.40            # confidence < 0.40: quarantine; do NOT commit

# Special overrides (engagement-profile can override per source type)
overrides:
  low_risk_exception_cents: 2500   # $25 no-receipt exception (SIG-016 Gap C)
  low_risk_provisional_date: 2026-06-03  # PROVISIONAL approval received via Turnberry (Brien, 2026-06-03)

  corporate_card_exception_cents: 7500  # $75 corporate card exception (existing policy)

---

# DDR-009: Always-on hosting substrate is GitHub Actions now, with an explicit cloud trajectory

## Context

Intent's deployment topology has had an unresolved load-bearing decision since at least
2026-05-16 (SIG-037, 48 days open at decision time): the processing pipeline, scheduled tasks,
and every L3/L4 autonomous-execution claim require a substrate that runs when the laptop is
closed or traveling. The 2026-07-03 cross-session-awareness work made the cost concrete: the
new witness-transcript-ingest preservation loop runs only while the desktop app is open, and
cron/launchd/scheduled-task reliability is structurally capped by laptop availability. Options
enumerated in CLAUDE.md since April: GitHub Actions, cloud service, dedicated machine.

## Decision

1. **GitHub Actions is the always-on substrate now.** Rationale (Brien, verbatim intent):
   it means the least amount of change because the system is already deep in git. Everything
   durable already lives in git repos (theparlor/*); Actions can run scheduled and event-driven
   jobs against those repos with no new infrastructure, no new auth model, and no new hosting
   relationship. Precedent already exists: intent-bot runs as GitHub Actions in theparlor/intent
   (reference_intent_repo_concurrency).
2. **An explicit trajectory to cloud is part of the decision, not a maybe.** Brien anticipates
   needing MCP space and cloud data space, benefits from placing applications in the cloud and
   reaching their functionality from anywhere, and intends to work with other developers. The
   Actions choice is the near-term substrate, chosen for delta-minimization, not the end state.
3. **Migration trigger conditions** (any one fires a fresh substrate DDR):
   - a second developer needs shared runtime access (not just repo access);
   - a workload exceeds the Actions execution model (long-running daemon, low-latency wake,
     job runtime beyond Actions limits);
   - an MCP server needs to be reachable from multiple clients/locations as a service.
4. **Partition axis adopted** (from SIG-037 proposed action 2): each pipeline stage gets
   classified on-demand vs must-be-always-on; only must-be-always-on stages migrate to Actions,
   on-demand stages stay local. First candidates to classify: witness-transcript-ingest,
   intake-drain, cast freshening nightly, the enrichment pipeline stages.

## Alternatives considered

- **Cloud service now.** Rejected for now: larger delta before it is needed; the multi-dev and
  MCP-space needs that justify it are anticipated, not present. Deliberately preserved as the
  named trajectory with trigger conditions above so this is a staging decision, not a rejection.
- **Dedicated machine.** Rejected: replaces one single point of availability (laptop) with
  another, plus hardware operations burden; no path to multi-dev collaboration.
- **Status quo (laptop-only).** Rejected: this is the condition that kept every L3/L4 always-on
  claim vapor for 48 days and gates the preservation loop shipped 2026-07-03.

## Consequences

- Scheduled work that must be reliable gets a git-reachable Actions expression; the app-open
  constraint on desktop scheduled tasks becomes a UX convenience layer, not the reliability layer.
- Secrets/credential handling moves into GitHub Actions secrets for any migrated stage;
  client-confidential content must NOT flow through Actions runners for NDA-scoped engagements
  without a per-engagement ruling (Work/ tree content stays out of Actions by default).
- The always-on partition classification becomes part of each product's operator doc.

## Validation criteria

- First migrated stage (recommend witness-transcript-ingest or intake-drain) runs on schedule
  for 14 consecutive days with the laptop's availability having no effect on run success.
- No NDA-scoped content appears in any Actions log or artifact (spot-audit after first 14 days).
- A fresh substrate DDR is written within one week of any migration trigger condition firing.

# "Laptop is not a strategy" — always-on hosting is the unresolved load-bearing decision

## Summary

The Rahul playbook (THM-005) independently states the exact failure Intent already names in its deployment-topology section: the processing pipeline must run somewhere always-on; "cron stops at 4am... nobody notices until Monday." Intent's CLAUDE.md explicitly flags this ("Brien's laptop goes offline during travel") and lists "Hosted deployment mode — always-on processing" as an open item. External corroboration raises the priority: every autonomous-execution claim Intent makes (L3/L4, autonomous pipeline) is undeliverable until this is resolved.

## Signal Value

This is not new information — it is *pressure on an existing unresolved decision*. The enrichment pipeline (Source → Dedup → Context → Trust Scorer → Classifier → Router) and any L3/L4 loop are vapor without a 24/7 substrate. The local-vs-always-on partition heuristic from the playbook (which stages run on-demand vs. must be always-on) is a usable organizing axis for the hosted-mode spec.

## Proposed Actions

1. Promote hosted-mode from open-item to a decision needing a DDR: GitHub Actions vs. cloud service vs. dedicated machine (options already enumerated in CLAUDE.md).
2. Use the local/always-on partition as the structuring axis: classify each pipeline stage by whether it can be on-demand.
3. Do NOT adopt the playbook's vendor-adjacent hosting recommendation or its cost math without independent sourcing.
</content>

# Orchestration debt — work-queue + scheduled-checkin gap surfaced during 7-tier Cast run

## Observation (Brien-direct, 2026-05-28 ~22:50 ET)

> "i feel like we skipped past some things we either previously built or intended to build in order to chain this all together. for example cortege was not properly functioning and only partial work was done. we need our work management queuing to be capable of holding the to do list and working through it and scheduling check ins etc to see if the blocked steps can kick off. i feel like we spent a whole week designing this and upgrading everything and now trying to do a refresh feels very manual"

## What today's session manifested

This session is the exemplar. Symptoms:

1. **Cortège dispatcher exits after dispatch, not after fetch.** Workers are spawn-chips that need Brien-click to start. No automatic worker-start; no automatic re-dispatch when batch completes. The Round 2 of 73 URLs sits queued until a human triggers the next Dispatcher spawn.
2. **Worker session = chip in UI.** If Brien isn't at his desk with the right Claude Desktop session focusable, the chips can't be clicked. The orchestration is gated on UI navigation, not on signal-driven execution.
3. **Budget decisions are manual interview questions.** I surfaced "T2 freshening-delta would exceed 35% — your call." That's an L0 micro-decision per session. The system has no policy for "Brien pre-authorized class X spending up to Y; auto-execute below threshold."
4. **L0 reminders are written into freeform response text.** "Don't forget linkedin-capture tomorrow" lives in my reply — no scheduled wake-up, no calendar trigger, no surfacable-at-next-session anchor. Same for BC1-BC5 ("when you return").
5. **Blocked-task auto-resume is absent.** T2 SYNTHESIS is gated on Cortège harvest landing. Today, that gating is expressed in prose ("see continuation signal"). There is no `wake_when: corpus-fresh-for(slug)` predicate that auto-dispatches synthesis when its precondition resolves.
6. **Cross-session task persistence is partial.** In-session TaskList exists; cross-session task continuity is via signal files + Brien-memory. No durable queue with "blocked → waiting → ready → in-progress → done" state.

## What we previously built or intended that would have helped

- **Intent framework** — captures decisions and signals, but doesn't EXECUTE them as a work plan.
- **chain_audit invariants** — surface drift, but don't drive auto-fix.
- **Cortège** — fetch fabric, but its scope stops at fetch; downstream synthesis/wiring is unowned.
- **Overwatch** — dashboard + reminder, not orchestrator (per memory).
- **signal-stream** — observability stream with closure-DoD, but no scheduler.
- **schedule / loop / ScheduleWakeup** — discrete timers, not a unified planner.
- **Forge composition engine** — composes skills, but doesn't run a queue.

## What's missing (proposed write-through — working name: `quartermaster`)

A sibling product at the **orchestration position**:

```
Plans / Decisions (Intent)          ← what to do, why
        ↓
Work Queue (quartermaster)          ← MISSING — when, by whom, with what budget, on what trigger
        ↓
Fetch Fabric (Cortège)              ← how to harvest
Synthesis (cast, voices, others)    ← how to think
Observability (signal-stream)       ← how we know it landed
```

Core verbs (port contract — first draft):
- `enqueue(task, when: ready|blocked, deps: [...], budget: bucket, wake_on: [predicates])` — submit work with explicit blockers + wake predicates
- `tick()` — re-evaluate queue; promote blocked → ready when predicates resolve; dispatch ready tasks per budget policy
- `surface(role: brien|claude, channel: signal|chip|notification)` — escalate items that need human action
- `checkin(cron|condition)` — schedule periodic re-evaluation (replaces ad-hoc launchd plists)
- `budget_authorize(class, cap, period)` — Brien pre-authorizes budget classes; auto-decisions below cap

Sibling-composability with existing products:
- Reads Intent decisions to know WHY a task exists (attribution chain)
- Subscribes to chain_audit invariant fires to auto-enqueue catch-net work
- Wraps Cortège for fetch tasks (Cortège becomes one of N workers)
- Writes signal-stream events for observability

## Why "symptom-repaired, upstream-pending" today

Today's symptom-fix:
- Manual interview rounds for decisions (worked but proved the gap)
- Manual budget threshold surfacing (worked but burns Brien attention)
- Gap-harvest agent dispatched as one-shot Sonnet job (correct execution; not durable mechanism)
- Cron plist scaffolded for metadata-freshening (per-decision launchd plist; not a planner)

Upstream control would be: `quartermaster` product scaffolded with the port contract above + chain_audit invariants for the three L4-discipline gaps + Brien-policy file defining pre-authorized budget classes.

## Concrete near-term remediation (this week)

1. **Brainstorm session for `quartermaster`** with Brien (60-90min, the kind of session Throughline / coherence-engineering work has gotten). Output: WS-DDR draft + INTENT.md scaffold.
2. **Inventory existing one-shot artifacts** that should become quartermaster-managed tasks: scheduled launchd plists, recurring cron entries, ad-hoc spawn_task chips, in-flight continuation signals.
3. **Carve scope**: what's quartermaster vs. what's Forge vs. what's Cortège. Boundary discipline matters here (recent pattern: WS-DDR-025 sibling-over-parent-child).
4. **Stub the port verbs as a no-op API** — even a YAML-on-disk implementation would be enough to start moving manual work into it.

## Closure DoD for this signal

- [x] Observation captured verbatim (Brien quote preserved)
- [x] Six symptoms enumerated with today's session as exemplar
- [x] Existing products inventoried (what helps, what doesn't)
- [x] Write-through proposed (`quartermaster` product + 5 port verbs + 3 candidate chain_audit invariants)
- [x] Honesty mark: status is `symptom-repaired, upstream-pending` — not resolved
- [x] Near-term remediation steps enumerated
- [ ] Brainstorm session scheduled (Brien action — see L0-reminder below)
- [ ] First INTENT.md + WS-DDR draft (downstream of brainstorm)
- [ ] No catch-net invariant exists today; three proposed — will be installed once quartermaster has minimum surface

## L0 reminders attached to this signal

- **L0-2 reminder (tomorrow morning):** activate `linkedin-capture` skill so 4 LinkedIn-blocked tasks can drain
- **L0-3 reminder (when Brien returns):** BC1-BC5 decision interview from SIG-PCU-PHASE-3-UPLIFT — 5 architectural calls (Schema v2 fate, SQLite substrate, observability shape, contract artifact depth, sibling-spec ratification sequencing)
- **L0-4 reminder (this week):** schedule `quartermaster` brainstorm session

- **L0-4 reminder (this week):** schedule `quartermaster` brainstorm session

+Brien greenlit quartermaster (register row 3, pending-decisions census). The 60-90min synchronous

+minimum surface.

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'premature-resolved-remediation',
  description: 'Classify and remediate the 22 premature-resolved findings across 15 signal files in the intent repo (Brien-authorized 2026-07-03, register row 4)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Classify and Remediate' },
    { title: 'Verify' },
  ],
}

const REPO = '/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/frameworks/intent'
const SIGDIR = `${REPO}/.intent/signals`

const FILES = [
  '2026-04-06-wiki-naming-wrong.md',
  '2026-04-12-karpathy-source-extraction-backlog.md',
  '2026-04-13-ke-mcp-write-back.md',
  '2026-04-13-trust-as-orientation-proxy.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-08-intent-framework-SIG-2.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-11-intent-site-SIG-1.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-21-invocation-gap-SIG-1.md',
  'SIG-035-intent-mcp-servers-hardcode-ports.md',
  'SIG-2026-05-28-stop-hook-check-6-trailing-observation.md',
  'SIG-2026-06-10-build-intake-gate-d4-scoped.md',
  'SIG-AUDIT-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-EXEC-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-EXEC-2026-06-03-methodology-coverage-invariant.md',
  'SIG-HOOK-OVERRIDE-META-INSTRUCTIONAL-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-INTENT-BROKEN-LINKS-FIXED-2026-05-20.md',
]

phase('Classify and Remediate')

const REMEDIATION_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    file: { type: 'string' },
    classification: { type: 'string', description: 'GENUINE-PREMATURE | GENUINELY-COMPLETE | CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE' },
    action_taken: { type: 'string' },
    evidence: { type: 'string' },
    checker_precision_note: { type: 'string', description: 'only if CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE: which regex/marker misfired and why' },
  },
  required: ['file', 'classification', 'action_taken', 'evidence'],
}

const results = await parallel(FILES.map(f => () =>
  agent(`You are remediating ONE signal file flagged by the closure-discipline write-boundary checker as PREMATURE-RESOLVED (status: resolved but body text contains markers like "follow-up", "needs X", "not yet", suggesting incomplete work). Brien authorized this remediation 2026-07-03 with fresh determination of what remediation should look like.

FILE: ${SIGDIR}/${f}

To see exactly which lines the checker flagged, run:
cd ${REPO} && servers/.venv/bin/python tools/closure_writeboundary_check.py 2>&1 | grep -A3 "${f}"

STEPS:
1. Read the signal file in full.
2. For each flagged marker, determine which of THREE cases applies:
   a. GENUINE-PREMATURE: the admitted follow-on work never happened. Verify by checking the repo state for whatever the body says was still needed (grep for the invariant/file/feature it mentions). If truly still open: change frontmatter status to "symptom-repaired, upstream-pending", ensure upstream_control_path / catch_mechanism / pipeline_survival keys exist and are HONEST (use "NONE TODAY" with a one-line gap description where nothing exists), and add a short "## Remediation note (2026-07-03)" section stating what remains open.
   b. GENUINELY-COMPLETE: the follow-on work DID land after the signal was written (verify with actual evidence: the file/invariant/fix it references exists in the repo now). Keep status: resolved, but REWRITE the flagged body line(s) to reflect completion with the evidence (e.g. "follow-up #1 (landed 2026-XX-XX: path/to/thing)"), so the language no longer admits incompleteness it no longer has. Ensure the three DoD keys are populated with the real paths.
   c. CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE: the marker is quoting a title, describing SOMEONE ELSE'S work, or using "needs" in a non-self-referential way. Do NOT contort the prose. Leave the text alone, note the misfire precisely in checker_precision_note (which regex, what innocent pattern it matched). Do not edit the checker itself.
3. Old signals (April) may predate the DoD-key convention entirely; treat missing keys as part of the remediation, not as evidence of guilt.
4. Make your edits directly to the file. Do NOT run git commands. Do NOT touch any other file.

Be conservative: when evidence is ambiguous between (a) and (b), choose (a), the honest downgrade. Report per the schema.`, { label: `remediate:${f.slice(0, 40)}`, phase: 'Classify and Remediate', schema: REMEDIATION_SCHEMA })
))

const done = results.filter(Boolean)
log(`Remediated ${done.length}/${FILES.length} files`)

phase('Verify')

const verify = await agent(`Re-run the closure-discipline write-boundary checker and report the residual state after a remediation pass.

Run: cd ${REPO} && servers/.venv/bin/python tools/closure_writeboundary_check.py 2>&1

Report: (1) total PREMATURE-RESOLVED findings remaining (was 22 before remediation), (2) which files still flag and whether each residual is a documented CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE from the remediation reports below or an unexplained miss, (3) confirm no signal file was left with a syntactically broken frontmatter (spot-check: head -30 on each of the 15 remediated files, valid YAML delimiters).

REMEDIATION REPORTS:
${JSON.stringify(done, null, 2).slice(0, 20000)}

Do not edit anything in this phase. Report only.`, { label: 'verify:rerun-checker', phase: 'Verify' })

return { remediations: done, verification: verify }

export const meta = {
  name: 'quartermaster-design',
  description: 'Artifact-first design for quartermaster (orchestration substrate: durable work queue + budget pre-authorization), replacing the greenlit brainstorm; produces the _intake scaffold + draft WS-DDR',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Inventory' },
    { title: 'Design' },
    { title: 'Judge' },
    { title: 'Scaffold' },
  ],
}

const WS = '/Users/brien/Workspaces'
const SIGNAL = `${WS}/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-05-28-orchestration-debt-work-queue-gap.md`

const CONTEXT = `Quartermaster is a greenlit (Brien, 2026-07-03) sibling product at the orchestration position:
Intent decisions (what/why) -> quartermaster work queue (when, by whom, what budget, what trigger) -> workers (Cortege fetch, cast/voices synthesis) -> signal-stream observability.
Founding signal (READ IT FIRST): ${SIGNAL} — contains the six manifest symptoms, the 5 draft port verbs (enqueue with deps/budget/wake_on, tick, surface, checkin, budget_authorize), 3 candidate chain_audit invariants (I-BLOCKED-TASK-HAS-WAKE, I-L0-ACTION-HAS-REMINDER, I-L4-ITEM-NOT-SOFT-QUEUED), and Brien's verbatim observation.
BINDING FRESH CONSTRAINT: DDR-009 (ratified 2026-07-03, ${WS}/Core/frameworks/intent/knowledge/decisions/DDR-009-always-on-hosting-substrate.md) — GitHub Actions is the always-on substrate now, cloud later with named triggers. Quartermaster's tick/checkin loop must be classifiable per DDR-009's on-demand vs always-on partition and must not assume a laptop-always-on daemon.
ALSO BINDING: WS-DDR-025 sibling-over-parent-child (quartermaster wraps Cortege as one worker class, never absorbs it); the 2026-07-03 liveness finding (entire status is the working liveness read-path; Loom heartbeats are broken, 149/1888; do not design a liveness mechanism, consume the existing one); the pending-decisions register at ${WS}/.context/PENDING_DECISIONS.md and overwatch Section 14 (quartermaster's surface() verb should feed that register, not invent a parallel surface); Brien's design canon: atomic and composable, smallest useful thing first, no new infrastructure where existing tooling suffices.`

phase('Inventory')
const inventory = await agent(`${CONTEXT}

YOUR TASK (founding signal remediation step 2): inventory every existing one-shot orchestration artifact that quartermaster should eventually manage. Concretely enumerate:
1. Scheduled tasks: ls /Users/brien/.claude/scheduled-tasks/ (each dir is one task; read each SKILL.md frontmatter for schedule + purpose, one line each)
2. launchd plists: ls ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ | grep -i "com.brien" plus any referenced in ${WS}/Core (grep -rl launchd --include="*.md" Core/frameworks/intent/ | head)
3. Continuation/handoff signals: grep -rl "continuation\\|NEXT-SESSION-CHARTER\\|handoff" ${WS}/Core/products/cast/.intent/ --include="*.md" | head -12 (count + classify, do not read all)
4. The reminders dir: ls ${WS}/.intent/reminders/
5. GitHub Actions already running: ls ${WS}/Core/frameworks/intent/.github/workflows/ and check theparlor/intent's intent-bot mention in ${WS}/.claude/../memory context is out of reach, so just note repo-local workflows.
Output a compact inventory table (artifact, class, trigger mechanism today, what quartermaster verb would own it), plus a count summary. This becomes design.md's migration appendix.`, { label: 'inventory', phase: 'Inventory' })

phase('Design')
const DESIGN_ANGLES = [
  { key: 'file-substrate', angle: 'MINIMAL FILE SUBSTRATE: queue state as YAML/JSONL on disk inside .intent/ conventions, tick() runs at SessionStart hooks + existing scheduled tasks, no daemon, no new process. Bias: zero new infrastructure, works offline, git-versioned queue state. Address honestly: how wake_on predicates get evaluated with no resident process, and what latency that implies.' },
  { key: 'actions-native', angle: 'GITHUB-ACTIONS-NATIVE: queue is GitHub Issues in a private repo (or repo files) with Actions cron as tick(), labels as states, issue comments as audit trail. Aligns directly with DDR-009 and the existing theparlor/intake pattern (issues + queued label + scheduled drain). Bias: always-on for free, survives laptop closure. Address honestly: NDA-content boundary (DDR-009 forbids client content in Actions), local-session integration (how a Claude Code session enqueues/consumes), and rate limits.' },
  { key: 'hybrid-ledger', angle: 'HYBRID LEDGER: local-first append-only queue ledger (jsonl, Witness-conservation-style) that syncs to git; tick() runs BOTH locally (SessionStart + scheduled tasks, low latency while laptop open) AND as an Actions cron fallback (heartbeat while laptop closed); budget_authorize as a policy YAML Brien ratifies once. Bias: the two tick surfaces cover each other. Address honestly: split-brain risk between local and Actions ticks, and idempotency discipline.' },
]

const DESIGN_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    name: { type: 'string' },
    core_mechanism: { type: 'string' },
    port_verbs: { type: 'string', description: 'the 5 verbs from the founding signal, made concrete for this substrate' },
    budget_preauth_design: { type: 'string' },
    smallest_useful_increment: { type: 'string', description: 'what ships in week 1 that already removes manual orchestration' },
    ddr009_partition: { type: 'string', description: 'which parts are on-demand vs always-on' },
    honest_weaknesses: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['name', 'core_mechanism', 'port_verbs', 'budget_preauth_design', 'smallest_useful_increment', 'ddr009_partition', 'honest_weaknesses'],
}

const designs = await parallel(DESIGN_ANGLES.map(d => () =>
  agent(`${CONTEXT}

Design quartermaster from this angle: ${d.angle}

Read the founding signal first. Produce a complete concrete design per the schema. Ground every claim in what actually exists (read files when unsure; do not invent tool capabilities).`, { label: `design:${d.key}`, phase: 'Design', schema: DESIGN_SCHEMA })
))

phase('Judge')

export const meta = {
  name: 'premature-resolved-remediation',
  description: 'Classify and remediate the 22 premature-resolved findings across 15 signal files in the intent repo (Brien-authorized 2026-07-03, register row 4)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Classify and Remediate' },
    { title: 'Verify' },
  ],
}

const REPO = '/Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/frameworks/intent'
const SIGDIR = `${REPO}/.intent/signals`

const FILES = [
  '2026-04-06-wiki-naming-wrong.md',
  '2026-04-12-karpathy-source-extraction-backlog.md',
  '2026-04-13-ke-mcp-write-back.md',
  '2026-04-13-trust-as-orientation-proxy.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-08-intent-framework-SIG-2.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-11-intent-site-SIG-1.md',
  'RETRO-2026-04-21-invocation-gap-SIG-1.md',
  'SIG-035-intent-mcp-servers-hardcode-ports.md',
  'SIG-2026-05-28-stop-hook-check-6-trailing-observation.md',
  'SIG-2026-06-10-build-intake-gate-d4-scoped.md',
  'SIG-AUDIT-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-EXEC-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-EXEC-2026-06-03-methodology-coverage-invariant.md',
  'SIG-HOOK-OVERRIDE-META-INSTRUCTIONAL-2026-05-20.md',
  'SIG-INTENT-BROKEN-LINKS-FIXED-2026-05-20.md',
]

phase('Classify and Remediate')

const REMEDIATION_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    file: { type: 'string' },
    classification: { type: 'string', description: 'GENUINE-PREMATURE | GENUINELY-COMPLETE | CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE' },
    action_taken: { type: 'string' },
    evidence: { type: 'string' },
    checker_precision_note: { type: 'string', description: 'only if CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE: which regex/marker misfired and why' },
  },
  required: ['file', 'classification', 'action_taken', 'evidence'],
}

const results = await parallel(FILES.map(f => () =>
  agent(`You are remediating ONE signal file flagged by the closure-discipline write-boundary checker as PREMATURE-RESOLVED (status: resolved but body text contains markers like "follow-up", "needs X", "not yet", suggesting incomplete work). Brien authorized this remediation 2026-07-03 with fresh determination of what remediation should look like.

FILE: ${SIGDIR}/${f}

To see exactly which lines the checker flagged, run:
cd ${REPO} && servers/.venv/bin/python tools/closure_writeboundary_check.py 2>&1 | grep -A3 "${f}"

STEPS:
1. Read the signal file in full.
2. For each flagged marker, determine which of THREE cases applies:
   a. GENUINE-PREMATURE: the admitted follow-on work never happened. Verify by checking the repo state for whatever the body says was still needed (grep for the invariant/file/feature it mentions). If truly still open: change frontmatter status to "symptom-repaired, upstream-pending", ensure upstream_control_path / catch_mechanism / pipeline_survival keys exist and are HONEST (use "NONE TODAY" with a one-line gap description where nothing exists), and add a short "## Remediation note (2026-07-03)" section stating what remains open.
   b. GENUINELY-COMPLETE: the follow-on work DID land after the signal was written (verify with actual evidence: the file/invariant/fix it references exists in the repo now). Keep status: resolved, but REWRITE the flagged body line(s) to reflect completion with the evidence (e.g. "follow-up #1 (landed 2026-XX-XX: path/to/thing)"), so the language no longer admits incompleteness it no longer has. Ensure the three DoD keys are populated with the real paths.
   c. CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE: the marker is quoting a title, describing SOMEONE ELSE'S work, or using "needs" in a non-self-referential way. Do NOT contort the prose. Leave the text alone, note the misfire precisely in checker_precision_note (which regex, what innocent pattern it matched). Do not edit the checker itself.
3. Old signals (April) may predate the DoD-key convention entirely; treat missing keys as part of the remediation, not as evidence of guilt.
4. Make your edits directly to the file. Do NOT run git commands. Do NOT touch any other file.

Be conservative: when evidence is ambiguous between (a) and (b), choose (a), the honest downgrade. Report per the schema.`, { label: `remediate:${f.slice(0, 40)}`, phase: 'Classify and Remediate', schema: REMEDIATION_SCHEMA })
))

const done = results.filter(Boolean)
log(`Remediated ${done.length}/${FILES.length} files`)

phase('Verify')

const verify = await agent(`Re-run the closure-discipline write-boundary checker and report the residual state after a remediation pass.

Run: cd ${REPO} && servers/.venv/bin/python tools/closure_writeboundary_check.py 2>&1

Report: (1) total PREMATURE-RESOLVED findings remaining (was 22 before remediation), (2) which files still flag and whether each residual is a documented CHECKER-FALSE-POSITIVE from the remediation reports below or an unexplained miss, (3) confirm no signal file was left with a syntactically broken frontmatter (spot-check: head -30 on each of the 15 remediated files, valid YAML delimiters).

REMEDIATION REPORTS:
${JSON.stringify(done, null, 2).slice(0, 20000)}

Do not edit anything in this phase. Report only.`, { label: 'verify:rerun-checker', phase: 'Verify' })

return { remediations: done, verification: verify }

export const meta = {
  name: 'quartermaster-design',
  description: 'Artifact-first design for quartermaster (orchestration substrate: durable work queue + budget pre-authorization), replacing the greenlit brainstorm; produces the _intake scaffold + draft WS-DDR',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Inventory' },
    { title: 'Design' },
    { title: 'Judge' },
    { title: 'Scaffold' },
  ],
}

const WS = '/Users/brien/Workspaces'
const SIGNAL = `${WS}/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-05-28-orchestration-debt-work-queue-gap.md`

const CONTEXT = `Quartermaster is a greenlit (Brien, 2026-07-03) sibling product at the orchestration position:
Intent decisions (what/why) -> quartermaster work queue (when, by whom, what budget, what trigger) -> workers (Cortege fetch, cast/voices synthesis) -> signal-stream observability.
Founding signal (READ IT FIRST): ${SIGNAL} — contains the six manifest symptoms, the 5 draft port verbs (enqueue with deps/budget/wake_on, tick, surface, checkin, budget_authorize), 3 candidate chain_audit invariants (I-BLOCKED-TASK-HAS-WAKE, I-L0-ACTION-HAS-REMINDER, I-L4-ITEM-NOT-SOFT-QUEUED), and Brien's verbatim observation.
BINDING FRESH CONSTRAINT: DDR-009 (ratified 2026-07-03, ${WS}/Core/frameworks/intent/knowledge/decisions/DDR-009-always-on-hosting-substrate.md) — GitHub Actions is the always-on substrate now, cloud later with named triggers. Quartermaster's tick/checkin loop must be classifiable per DDR-009's on-demand vs always-on partition and must not assume a laptop-always-on daemon.
ALSO BINDING: WS-DDR-025 sibling-over-parent-child (quartermaster wraps Cortege as one worker class, never absorbs it); the 2026-07-03 liveness finding (entire status is the working liveness read-path; Loom heartbeats are broken, 149/1888; do not design a liveness mechanism, consume the existing one); the pending-decisions register at ${WS}/.context/PENDING_DECISIONS.md and overwatch Section 14 (quartermaster's surface() verb should feed that register, not invent a parallel surface); Brien's design canon: atomic and composable, smallest useful thing first, no new infrastructure where existing tooling suffices.`

phase('Inventory')
const inventory = await agent(`${CONTEXT}

YOUR TASK (founding signal remediation step 2): inventory every existing one-shot orchestration artifact that quartermaster should eventually manage. Concretely enumerate:
1. Scheduled tasks: ls /Users/brien/.claude/scheduled-tasks/ (each dir is one task; read each SKILL.md frontmatter for schedule + purpose, one line each)
2. launchd plists: ls ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ plus any referenced in grep -rl launchd ${WS}/Core/frameworks/intent --include="*.md" (head only)
3. Continuation/handoff signals: grep -rl "continuation\\|NEXT-SESSION-CHARTER\\|handoff" ${WS}/Core/products/cast/.intent/ --include="*.md" | head -12 (count + classify, do not read all)
4. The reminders dir: ls ${WS}/.intent/reminders/
5. Repo-local GitHub Actions: ls ${WS}/Core/frameworks/intent/.github/workflows/
Output a compact inventory table (artifact, class, trigger mechanism today, what quartermaster verb would own it), plus a count summary. This becomes design.md's migration appendix.`, { label: 'inventory', phase: 'Inventory' })

phase('Design')
const DESIGN_ANGLES = [
  { key: 'file-substrate', angle: 'MINIMAL FILE SUBSTRATE: queue state as YAML/JSONL on disk inside .intent/ conventions, tick() runs at SessionStart hooks + existing scheduled tasks, no daemon, no new process. Bias: zero new infrastructure, works offline, git-versioned queue state. Address honestly: how wake_on predicates get evaluated with no resident process, and what latency that implies.' },
  { key: 'actions-native', angle: 'GITHUB-ACTIONS-NATIVE: queue is GitHub Issues in a private repo (or repo files) with Actions cron as tick(), labels as states, issue comments as audit trail. Aligns directly with DDR-009 and the existing theparlor/intake pattern (issues + queued label + scheduled drain). Bias: always-on for free, survives laptop closure. Address honestly: NDA-content boundary (DDR-009 forbids client content in Actions), local-session integration (how a Claude Code session enqueues/consumes), and rate limits.' },
  { key: 'hybrid-ledger', angle: 'HYBRID LEDGER: local-first append-only queue ledger (jsonl, Witness-conservation-style) that syncs to git; tick() runs BOTH locally (SessionStart + scheduled tasks, low latency while laptop open) AND as an Actions cron fallback (heartbeat while laptop closed); budget_authorize as a policy YAML Brien ratifies once. Bias: the two tick surfaces cover each other. Address honestly: split-brain risk between local and Actions ticks, and idempotency discipline.' },
]

const DESIGN_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    name: { type: 'string' },
    core_mechanism: { type: 'string' },
    port_verbs: { type: 'string', description: 'the 5 verbs from the founding signal, made concrete for this substrate' },
    budget_preauth_design: { type: 'string' },
    smallest_useful_increment: { type: 'string', description: 'what ships in week 1 that already removes manual orchestration' },
    ddr009_partition: { type: 'string', description: 'which parts are on-demand vs always-on' },
    honest_weaknesses: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['name', 'core_mechanism', 'port_verbs', 'budget_preauth_design', 'smallest_useful_increment', 'ddr009_partition', 'honest_weaknesses'],
}

const designs = await parallel(DESIGN_ANGLES.map(d => () =>
  agent(`${CONTEXT}

Design quartermaster from this angle: ${d.angle}

Read the founding signal first. Produce a complete concrete design per the schema. Ground every claim in what actually exists (read files when unsure; do not invent tool capabilities).`, { label: `design:${d.key}`, phase: 'Design', schema: DESIGN_SCHEMA })
))

phase('Judge')
const validDesigns = designs.filter(Boolean)
const judgment = await agent(`${CONTEXT}

Three competing quartermaster designs follow. Judge them as a panel would: score each (1-10) on (a) composability with the existing product fabric (Intent, Cortege as worker, Loom/entire-status liveness, overwatch Section 14 surface, Witness observability), (b) smallest-useful-first (does week 1 already remove real manual orchestration), (c) DDR-009 fit (survives laptop closure without violating the NDA boundary), (d) failure-mode honesty (split-brain, wedged queues, silent stalls). Then pick a winner and name exactly which elements to graft from the losers.

DESIGNS:
${JSON.stringify(validDesigns, null, 2)}

INVENTORY (migration reality-check, from a sibling agent):
${typeof inventory === 'string' ? inventory.slice(0, 8000) : JSON.stringify(inventory).slice(0, 8000)}

Output: scores table, winner, graft list, and the 2-4 design questions that genuinely need Brien (only ones the panel cannot settle from canon; everything settleable, settle).`, { label: 'judge', phase: 'Judge' })

phase('Scaffold')
const scaffold = await agent(`${CONTEXT}

You are writing the quartermaster intake scaffold. Per the Workspaces filing digest, new pre-product work goes to ${WS}/Core/products/_intake/2026-07-03-quartermaster/ with NOTICE.md + design.md + STATUS.md. Create that directory and write:

1. NOTICE.md: the founding observation (quote Brien verbatim from the signal), the six symptoms, why now (greenlit 2026-07-03, DDR-009 just landed).
2. design.md: the winning design with grafts applied, the 5 port verbs concretized, budget-preauth policy shape, DDR-009 partition table, migration appendix from the inventory, the 3 chain_audit invariants as the catch-net plan, and an honest failure-modes section. Attribute the judged-out alternatives in a short "alternatives considered" section.
3. STATUS.md: status pre-product-intake, greenlit-by Brien 2026-07-03, next gate = Brien reacts to draft WS-DDR, then smallest-useful-increment build.
4. draft-ws-ddr.md: a WS-DDR draft (Context / Decision / Alternatives Considered / Consequences / Validation Criteria) framed as PROPOSED, awaiting Brien ratification, for the structural decision: quartermaster exists as a sibling product at the orchestration position with this substrate choice. Include the open design questions from the judge verbatim as "open riders" so ratification can settle them.

JUDGMENT:
${typeof judgment === 'string' ? judgment.slice(0, 12000) : JSON.stringify(judgment).slice(0, 12000)}

WINNING + LOSING DESIGNS (full):
${JSON.stringify(validDesigns, null, 2).slice(0, 16000)}

INVENTORY:
${typeof inventory === 'string' ? inventory.slice(0, 8000) : JSON.stringify(inventory).slice(0, 8000)}

Hard style rules: YAML frontmatter (title/type/maturity/created) on every md file; lowercase-hyphenated filenames; NO em-dashes, en-dashes, arrow glyphs, or ellipsis characters anywhere; absolute paths written bare. Do NOT run git commands. Return a one-paragraph summary of what you wrote plus the list of open riders for Brien.`, { label: 'scaffold', phase: 'Scaffold' })

return { inventory, designs: validDesigns, judgment, scaffold }

content.text:
**TL;DR:** All four dispositions are recorded in their signal files, committed, and the register just dropped from 10 open rows to 6. The two work streams you authorized are executing now as parallel background workflows. **Status:** decisions landed; execution in flight; nothing further gated on you this turn. **Your move:** none until the workflows report back, then the quartermaster draft WS-DDR will have a short list of open riders for your ratification.

**What each ruling became:**

**Row 1, $25 exception (was 51 days old):** recorded in the fieldbook signal and config with your scope predicate intact: approved for all-in cost-model engagements where the client is passed no expenses and sees no hours; pass-through engagements like Turnberry/Subaru (T&E reimbursed) stay at the strict threshold, the ruling does not touch them. One nuance preserved: fieldbook's compliance engine reads the threshold from each engagement's own policy file, so the remaining work is purely mechanical, bumping the threshold in all-in engagement policies only. Committed in the fieldbook repo.

**Row 2, always-on hosting (was 48 days old):** promoted to DDR-009 in the intent repo, ratified with your reasoning verbatim: GitHub Actions now because you're already deep in git and it's the least change, and the cloud direction (MCP space, cloud data space, multi-dev access) written in as the named trajectory with three concrete migration triggers, not a vague someday: a second dev needing shared runtime, a workload exceeding the Actions execution model, or an MCP server needing multi-client reach. One consequence worth your awareness: the DDR forbids NDA-scoped Work/ content from flowing through Actions runners by default, so client-engagement automation stays local unless you rule otherwise per engagement.

**Row 4, premature-resolved remediation:** your instinct about fresh determination was right, the population grew from 18 findings across 9 files (May 30) to 22 across 15 today. The remediation workflow is fanning one agent per file with a three-way classification: genuinely-premature gets an honest downgrade with populated DoD keys, genuinely-complete-since gets its body language updated with evidence, and checker false-positives get documented precisely without contorting the prose (so the checker's precision can be tuned separately, not gamed). A verify phase re-runs the checker afterward and reports residuals. No commits until I review the verification.

**Row 3, quartermaster:** the steps you asked about, made concrete. The greenlit 60-90 minute brainstorm converts to artifact-first: an inventory agent is cataloging every one-shot orchestration artifact that should become queue-managed (scheduled tasks, launchd plists, continuation signals, the reminders dir), three design agents are building competing substrates (pure-local files, GitHub-Actions-native per your fresh DDR-009, and a hybrid local-plus-Actions ledger), a judge panel scores them against composability, smallest-useful-first, and the NDA boundary, and a scaffold agent writes the intake package at /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/_intake/2026-07-03-quartermaster/ including the draft WS-DDR. You react to artifacts instead of sitting a session; anything the panel can't settle from canon comes to you as named riders on the draft. One correction for the record: the census had mis-attributed the founding signal's path to cast; it actually lives at /Users/brien/Workspaces/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-05-28-orchestration-debt-work-queue-gap.md, and the register now says so.

Both workflows will notify on completion; I'll review the remediation verification before committing the intent-repo changes, and hand you the quartermaster riders when the scaffold lands.
